title: Lorelei of the Red Mist
author: Leigh Douglass Brackett, Ray Bradbury
description: Ray Starke, a small time criminal, crashes his shuttle while trying to escape pursuit after robbing a payroll worth millions of credits. When he comes to there is an alien woman telling him he's dying but she will put his consciousness in another body and help him escape using telepathy. - Summary by kirk202

title: Mummy! A Tale of the Twenty-Second Century
author: Jane C. Loudon
description: The Mummy!: Or a Tale of the Twenty-Second Century is about a wise Egyptian mummy who is reanimated far in the future. With a different take on what lies ahead for civilization inspired from the exciting developments of the era, it is a strange, entertaining story and an early science fiction work by a woman novelist. (Summary by A. Gramour)

title: Chaos To Calm
author: Amy Dalwood-Fairbanks
description: Parenting a neurodivergent child can feel exhausting, isolating and misunderstood. Chaos to Calm is a compassionate podcast for parents navigating anxiety, sensory overwhelm, meltdowns, shutdowns, autistic burnout and school avoidance. Hosted by paediatric hypnotherapist and neurodivergent parent Amy Dalwood-Fairbanks, each episode offers practical, low-demand support to help you understand what’s happening beneath your child’s behaviour. You’ll explore shared calm, emotional safety and nervous system-led parenting, without blame or judgement. Follow Chaos to Calm and join the Magic Minds Parents’ Hub for more gentle, realistic family support. The link is in the show notes.

title: Tenth Planet
author: Joseph Samachson
description: The population explosion has reached a point of grave danger for the Solar System. The inhabitants of all nine planets, cramped together in layers of airless living quarters, are becoming ill. If something isn’t done, the species will soon die out! Captain Future has a solution. He has devised an ingenious means of building a new planet. His idea is hailed by the government and the people alike. Only one man secretly opposes the plan, Harley Brooks, the corrupt interplanetary tycoon, has his own plans for a Solar System Empire. He must now see that Captain Future disappears — permanently! - Summary by Original book cover

title: Revolutions of Time
author: Jonathan Dunn
description: The Canitaurs, who believe that the past is the key to the future, and the Zards, who believe that the past is just the past, are at war and each believes that a redeemer will arrive and propel them to victory. What role Jehu, who has been summoned to the island of Daem, will play in stopping these hostilities is unclear but he soon learns that he will determine not just the outcome of the war but the fate of all humanity.

title: Subspace Survivors
author: Lee Hawkins Garby
description: A team of space travelers are caught in a subspace accident which, up to now, no one has ever survived. But some of the survivors of the Procyon are not ordinary travelers. Their psi abilities allow them to see things before they happen. But will it be enough? Smith's story "Subspace Survivors" first appeared in the July 1960 issue of the magazine Astounding.

title: Slave Planet
author: Laurence M. Janifer
description: Fruyling's World ... rich in the metals that kept the Terran Confederation going—one vital link in a galaxy-wide civilization. But the men of Fruyling's World lived on borrowed time, knowing that slavery was outlawed throughout the Confederation—and that only the slave labor of the reptilian natives could produce the precious metals the Confederation needed! As the first hints of the truth about Fruyling's World emerge, the tension becomes unbearable—to be resolved only in the shattering climax of this fast-paced, thought-provoking story. - Summary by Book Intro

title: Centaurians
author: L. D. Biagi
description: Published in 1911, 15 years before the first verified discovery of the North Pole and in the same year when F. Cook published a memoir claiming his own discovery of the Pole, this short SciFi about the discovery of the Pole and the civilisation on the other side which is supposedly 6 centuries in advance compared to our own, was doomed to sink in the unknown from the beginning on. But reading it a century later, this SciFi shows its own charm by conveying the general sentiment in the society short before the big discovery and the motivation behind the continued exploration despite the associated hardships. Some predictions, such as the manmade diamonds, even came true very recently, while some others are overtaken by the real advancement in science already. - Summary by gyllila

title: Countdown
author: Kurt Becker. S. J.
description: The first flight to outer space became an actual fact – Mars would be the first stop. But before the spaceship took off, two insane enemies almost succeeded in preventing the departure. This science fiction story for teens was written by a Catholic priest. (Summary from the original jacket and Maria Therese)

title: Nequa or The Problem of the Ages
author: Jack Adams
description: Nequa or The Problem of the Ages is one of the first feminist science fiction books published in the United States. It was first serialized in the newspaper Equity, and two editions were published in Topeka, Kansas in 1900. The voyage described in Nequa is to the Arctic. The captain of the ship is amazed to find that when they reach a certain spot, which should be close to the North Pole, the compass shows that the ship is suddenly traveling south. Actually they have sailed into the interior of the earth, where they meet the Altrurians, a people who have developed into a more cooperative society than those of the outer earth. Individual Altrurians describe how the evolution of their society took place. These explanations reflect the Populist thought of the time, with definite feminist proclivities, thereby providing the tone of a political novel. While the book was published with the pseudonym of Jack Adams, it was copyrighted under the names Alcanoan A. Grigsby and Mary P. Lowe, who are presumed to be the true authors. - Summary by Jeff Burke

title: Bravo & Books
author: Jessica Hill
description: A podcast by 2 cousins who connect on their shared love of Bravo TV and reading!

title: R.U.R. (Rossum’s Universal Robots)
author: Karel Čapek
description: Helena Glory, as the daughter of a major industrial power's president, is a woman on a mission. She faces the island factory of Rossum's Universal Robots, the world's leading company in robotic engineering. She is convinced that these new creations called robots they make are deserving of rights like humans do. Everyone else is convinced robots are nothing more than tools for human use. Is it so, or is a robot rebellion becoming a more likely prospect as the robots start to seem more intelligent than first thought? First performed in English in 1922, R.U.R. is most notable for being the play that introduced the word "robot" into the English language and one of the popular early examples of the science fiction genre onstage. (Mary Kay)Cast List: Narrator: John TrevithickHelena Glory / Robot Helena: Sarah TerryHarry Domin / Primus: ToddHWDr. Gall: Barry StreifertMr. Fabry: James CallaghanDr. Hallemeier / Consul Busman: SteveTonerMr. Alquist: Beth ThomasNana: KHandRadius: T. LayneSulla / First Robot: Tina NuzziMarius / Fourth Robot: Rosslyn CarlyleA Servant: Etel BussSecond Robot / Third Robot: Kitty Kay / Mary Kay

title: Cosmic Courtship
author: Julian Hawthorne
description: Mary Faust, a brilliant scientist, has developed a machine that can allow the conscious human soul to explore the cosmos! Her promising young assistant Miriam Mayne has accidentally transferred her consciousness to Saturn, where she falls under the enchantment of an evil sorcerer! Jack Paladin, her love, sets out after her on a thrilling celestial journey to the ringed planet! Swashbuckling adventure and high romance await in Julian Hawthorne's The Cosmic Courtship! - Summary from back of book

title: Astounding Stories 02, February 1930
author: Hugh B. Cave, Charles Willard Diffin, Sophie Wenzel Ellis, Captain S. P. Meek, Anthony Pelcher, Victor Rousseau, Harl Vincent
description: This is the second issue of the classic science fiction Astounding Magazine. It contains the finale of The Beetle Horde by Victor Rousseau, as well as stories by Harl Vincent, Charles Willard Diffin, Hugh B. Cave, Sophie Wenzel Ellis, Sterner St. Paul, Anthony Pelcher and Captain S. P. Meek. (Summary by Alan Winterrowd)

title: Exile from Space
author: Judith Merril
description: “Who was this strange girl who had been born in this place—and still it wasn't her home? ‘They’ worried about the impression she'd make”. “They” worried she would go and never come back. She was returning to a place that was not hers, with ways of living that she didn’t understand. “They” failed to prepare her for what happened. - Summary by Jenn Broda and The Author

title: Under the Global Umbrella
author: Diversity Umbella
description:
Welcome to Under the Global Umbrella — a podcast celebrating the people and organisations driving real change in diversity, equity and inclusion across the world

Created by Diversity Umbrella, this series shines a spotlight on the incredible winners and changemakers from the British Diversity Awards, the European Diversity Awards, the Rainbow Honours, and the Employee Network Awards.

Through honest conversations and powerful stories, we go beyond the headlines to explore the impact behind the accolades — sharing lived experiences, bold leadership, and the ideas shaping more inclusive workplaces and communities.

Because recognition matters — but understanding the journey behind it matters even more.

title: Hunters Out of Space
author: Joseph E. Kelleam
description: Originally published in the May, 1960 issue of Amazing Science Fiction Stories. Jack Odin has returned to the world of Opal, the world inside our own world, only to find it in ruins. Many of his friends are gone, the world is flooded, and the woman he swore to protect has been taken by Grim Hagen to the stars. Jack must save her, but the difficulties are great and his allies are few. (Summary by Elliott Miller)

title: Journey in Other Worlds: A Romance of the Future
author: John Jacob Astor IV
description: A Journey in Other Worlds: A Romance of the Future is a science fiction novel by John Jacob Astor IV, published in 1894. The book offers a fictional account of life in the year 2000. It contains abundant speculation about technological invention, including descriptions of a world-wide telephone network, solar power, air travel, space travel to the planets Saturn and Jupiter, and terraforming engineering projects — damming the Arctic Ocean, and adjusting the Earth's axial tilt (by the Terrestrial Axis Straightening Company). In Astor's novel, the future United States is a multi-continental superpower. European nations have been taken over by socialist governments, which have sold most of their African colonies to the U.S.; and Canada, Mexico, and the countries of South America have requested annexation. Race conflict is a thing of the past, since the "dark elements" of the American hegemony have died out. Space travel is achieved by linking an airship to a comet. Jupiter proves to be a jungle world, with flesh-eating plants, vampire bats, giant snakes and mastodons, and flying lizards. The Americans discover a wealth of exploitable resources: iron, silver, gold, lead, copper, coal, and oil. Saturn, in contrast, is an ancient world of silent spirits. The spirit beings provide the explorers with foresight of their own deaths. (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: Astounding Stories 19, July 1931
author: Harry Bates, Ray Cummings, H. Thompson Rich, Nat Schachner, Jack Williamson, Arthur L. Zagat
description: THE DOOM FROM PLANET 4 JACK WILLIAMSON A Ray of Fire, Green, Mysterious, Stabs Through the Night to Dan on His Ship. It Leads Him to an Island of Unearthly Peril. THE HANDS OF ATEN H. G. WINTER Out of the Solid Ice Craig Hews Three Long-Frozen Egyptians and Is at Once Caught Up into Amazing Adventure. (A Complete Novelette.) THE DIAMOND THUNDERBOLT H. THOMPSON RICH Locked in a Rocket and Fired into Space! Such Was the Fate which Awaited Young Stoddard at the End of the Diamond Trail! THE SLAVE SHIP FROM SPACE A. R. HOLMES Three Kidnapped Earthlings Show Xantra of the Tillas How "Docile" Earth Slaves Can Be. THE REVOLT OF THE MACHINES NAT SCHACHNER AND ARTHUR L. ZAGAT Something in the Many-Faceted Mind of the Master Machine Spurs It to Diabolical Revolt Against the Authority of Its Human Masters. THE EXILE OF TIME RAY CUMMINGS Only Near the End of the World Does Fate Catch Up with Tugh, the Cripple Who Ran Amuck Through Time. (Conclusion.) THE READERS' CORNER ALL OF US A Meeting Place for Readers of Astounding Stories.

title: Card Trick
author: John Berryman
description: The Psi Lodge had their ways and means of applying pressure, when pressure was needed. But the peculiar talent this fellow showed was one that even they'd never heard of...! - Summary by John Berryman

title: Devil-Ray
author: Joel Martin Nichols, Jr.
description: George Ferris is a master thief with amnesia. He and his two partners in crime concoct a plan to break into the famed Castle Blennerhof in a search for priceless jewels. While on the job, they hear the roar of a plane and a mysterious beam of light emanates from the sky onto one of his partners, killing him instantly. And this is just the beginning of their strange and troubled caper. - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Queen of Appalachia
author: Joe H. Borders
description: A Lost Race novel set unusually in the eastern USA, where a civilization made up of descendants of early American pioneers has established an arcadian, monarchical Utopia supported by advanced Technology.

title: Time Dissolver
author: Jerry Sohl
description: WAS IT MENTAL SUICIDE...OR MENTAL MURDER? He awoke one morning in a strange motel beside a beautiful woman he'd never seen before. The clothes on the chair seemed to fit, but he didn't recognize them.... Upon investigation he found eleven years of history had passed - the Korean War, Ike's election, Stalin's death - without his knowing it! WHAT TERRIBLE THING WAS HE MADE TO FORGET? - Summary on Inside front cover of Avon Original edition

title: First on the Moon
author: Jeff Sutton
description: The four men had been scrutinized, watched, investigated, and intensively trained for more than a year. They were the best men to be found for that first, all-important flight to the Moon--the pioneer manned rocket that would give either the East or the West control over the Earth.Yet when the race started, Adam Crag found that he had a saboteur among his crew ... a traitor! Such a man could give the Reds possession of Luna, and thereby dominate the world it circled.Any one of the other three could be the hidden enemy, and if he didn't discover the agent soon--even while they were roaring on rocket jets through outer space--then Adam Crag, his expedition, and his country would be destroyed! (Summary by Jeff Sutton)

title: Rat Race
author: Jay Franklin
description: When an atomic explosion destroys the battleship Alaska, Lt. Commander Frank Jacklin returns to consciousness in New York and is shocked to find himself in the body of Winnie Tompkins, a dissolute stock-broker. Unable to explain his real identity, Jacklin attempts to fit into Tompkins' way of life. Complications develop when Jacklin gets involved with Tompkins' wife, his red-haired mistress and his luscious secretary. Three too many women for Jacklin to handle. His foreknowledge of the Alaska sinking and other top secret matters plunges him into a mad world of intrigue and excitement in Washington—that place where anything can happen and does! Where is the real Tompkins is a mystery explained in the smashing climax. Completely delightful, wholly provocative, the Rat Race is a striking novel of the American Scene. - Summary by Book Intro

title: Preparing for the Zombie Apocalypse
author: James Lawrence
description:
The podcast built around one simple idea;
If you're not prepared for the Zombie Apocalypse, you're probably not prepared for real life either....because the Apocalypse is just real life with the volume turned all the way up.

title: Breaking Point
author: James Edwin Gunn
description: Machines are infallible. Humans are not. The crew of The Ambassador knew their ship could not possibly fail, but what of themselves? And how could they defend against an unknown enemy with unknown motives and superhuman abilities, intent on driving each one of them beyond his own personal breaking point? - Summary by Summary by Peter Eastman

title: Thing in the Attic (version 2)
author: James B. Blish
description: "Honath and his fellow arch-doubters did not believe in the Giants, and for this they were cast into Hell. And when survival depended upon unwavering faith in their beliefs, they saw that there were Giants, after all...." The Thing in the Attic is a Science Fiction story by James Blish with a well developed story line and great characters that you find yourself pulling for as they struggle to understand and survive in 'hell'. Do they have tails? yes they do. Do they live in the treetops? you bet they do. Are they 'people'? Well, I will let you decide that. The strange beings and environment that you expect from Blish are all here and they challenge you to adapt your thinking to keep up with the action. And of course there is an ending that is definitely not expected. Listen and enjoy this story of a strange world far in the future of the human race. - Summary by Phil Chenevert

title: Flying Eyes
author: J. Hunter Holly
description: The National Guardsmen waited for the Flying Eyes, restlessly fingering their rifles and machine guns. Then they came-eight of them-diving straight at the men. The men opened fire. One of the Eyes shot upward, blood and tears streaming from its wound. As the men watched, a glaze spread over the wound drawing the hole together. The Eye was healed and whole again-and it gazed at the men with the same sinister expression they had seen before. Then the full horror of what they had witnessed hit them. Nothing could stop these weird, disem- bodied creatures who were daily hypnotizing thousands of people and leading them into dark oblivion and threatening to blanket the Earth with a new, alien kind of civilization. - Summary by Inside book cover

title: Hampdenshire Wonder
author: J. D. Beresford
description: By the age of three, Victor Stott's intellectual powers exceed those of any known human. He is the Hampdenshire Wonder, son of an extraordinary cricketer, and a puzzle to all who come in contact with him. J. D. Beresford's early science fiction novel, written in style of his friend H. G. Wells, tells the story of the wonder's brief life and his influence on those who care for him. - Summary by Phil Benson

title: Deadly City
author: Ivar Jorgensen
description: You’re all alone in a deserted city. You walk down an empty street, yearning for the sight of one living face — one moving figure. Then you see a man on a corner and you know your terror has only begun. Frank Brooks wakes up in Chicago after being drugged in an evacuated city, apparently surrounded by aliens... - Summary by The Narrator

title: Cartels Jungle
author: Irving E. Cox, Jr.
description: In most ideally conceived Utopias the world as it exists is depicted as a mushrooming horror of maladjustment, cruelty and crime. In this startlingly original short novel that basic premise is granted, but only to pave the way for an approach to Utopia over a highway of the mind so daringly unusual we predict you'll forget completely that you're embarking on a fictional excursion into the future by one of the most gifted writers in the field. And that forgetfulness will be accompanied by the startling realization that Irving E. Cox has a great deal more than a storyteller's magic to impart.

title: Twelve Times Zero
author: Howard Carleton Browne
description: It was a love-triangle murder that made today's headlines but the answer lay hundreds of thousands of light years away! - Summary by If: Worlds of Science Fiction, March 1952

title: Currently Spiraling
author: Jo De La Rosa
description:
Currently Spiraling is a podcast about what happens when life doesn't go according to plan.
Hosted by Real Housewives of Orange County original cast member, Jo De La Rosa, each episode explores the plot twists, setbacks, and unexpected turns we never saw coming.
From fertility journeys and career pivots to relationships, money, aging, self-worth, and starting over, Currently Spiraling is about the things women quietly spiral about, but rarely say out loud.
Through candid conversations, thoughtful interviews, and personal stories, Jo explores the moments that leave us questioning everything and wondering what comes next. Listeners leave feeling less alone, a little more equipped, and reminded that there is no perfect timeline.
No pretending to have it all figured out. No pressure to have all the answers. Just honest conversations with humor, perspective, hope, and a little unhinged energy.
Whether you're trying to conceive, navigating a major life transition, questioning your path, grieving the life you thought you'd have by now, or simply adjusting to a chapter you never expected, this show is your reminder that you're not behind. You're simply in the middle of your story.
New episodes every Wednesday.

title: Republic Without A President, and Other Stories
author: Herbert Ward
description: Set in the 1890's "A Republic Without a President and Other Stories" is a collection of short stories that looks at American politics through the lens of science fiction. - Summary by Kristin Hand

title: Man Who Ended War
author: Hollis Godfrey
description: Jim Orrington, news reporter, is at the office when the Secretary of War brings in a letter--mostly likely a prank--that demands all the nations of the world to disarm in one year or have all their battleships destroyed. This letter, signed "The man who will stop all war", is ignored by Orrington's fellow reporters and by the U.S. government, but he decides to dig deeper. With the help of Tom and Dorothy Haldane, Jim Orrington embarks on an adventure around the world trying to stop the man who is determined to end all fighting before he catapults the entire world into chaos and war! - Summary by Adele de Pignerolles

title: Toffee Takes A Trip
author: Henry Farrell
description: Marc Pillsworth decided he needed a vacation—so he went on a trip. But where Marc went, Toffee followed—with trouble. - Summary by Fantastic Adventures July 1947

title: The Market Maven
author: Sarah Dylan Jensen
description: Peeling back the layers of Farmers Markets – diving into the “WHY” behind the 10x10 tents, showcasing the roots behind the organizations; and connecting those in our communities through education, stories and more. A Market Manager's job is weird, and I want to share more about it. Bringing produce and powerful stories to the people.

title: Astounding Stories 20, August 1931
author: Harry Bates, Ralph Milne Farley, Captain S. P. Meek, R. F. Starzl, Harl Vincent
description: This issue contains THE DANGER FROM THE DEEP by Ralph Milne Farley Marooned on the Sea-Floor, His Hoisting Cable Cut, Young Abbot Is Left at the Mercy of the Man-Sharks [BROOD OF THE DARK MOON PART 1 .We are not inlcuding this as this is the last full issue available. All 4 episodes are available in our catalogue]. IF THE SUN DIED by R.F Starzl Tens of Millenniums After the Death of the Sun There Comes a Young Man Who Dares to Open the Frozen Gate of Subterranea. THE MIDGET FROM THE ISLAND by H.G. Winter Garth Howard, Prey to Half the Animals of the Forest, Fights Valiantly to Regain His Lost Five Feet of Size. A Complete Novelette. "Jazzing up the Universe" A Science Fact THE MOON WEED by Harl Vincent Unwittingly the Traitor of the Earth, Van Pits Himself Against the Inexorably Tightening Web of Plant-Beasts He Has Released from the Moon. THE PORT OF MISSING PLANES by Captain S.P. Meek In the Underground Caverns of the Selom, Dr. Bird Once Again Locks Wills with the Subversive Genius, Saranoff. "A Classification of the Universe " A Science Fact THE READERS' CORNER ALL OF US A Meeting Place for Readers of Astounding Stories. " A living disembodied heart" - Summary by Annise

title: Astounding Stories 14, February 1931
author: Harry Bates, Charles Willard Diffin, Desmond Winter Hall, F. V. W. Mason, Captain S. P. Meek
description: This issue includes "Werewolves of War" by D. W. Hall, "The Tentacles from Below" by Anthony Gilmore, "The Black Lamp" by Captain S. P. Meek, "Phalanxes of Atlans" by F. V. W. Mason, and continues with "The Pirate Planet" by Charles W. Diffin, - Summary by Bill Boerst

title: Biddy and the Silver Man
author: Harlan Ellison
description: A man came out of the sky and they took him and hanged him from the nearest tree thinking that they lynched a devil. But perhaps they crucified a saint instead—there in the beauty of the desert. And what place could be more worthy of being called a second Calvary? - Summary by Fantastic, February 1957

title: Eduvation
author: Hadas
description:
🎧 Eduvation — Turning Ideas into Scalable Learning Product. When education meets innovation
Hosted by Hadas Yehezkely, EdTech advisor and product marketingstrategist, Eduvation is for educators, founders, and creators who want to turn ideas into scalable courses, platforms, and learning products.
Each episode breaks down what actually works in learning design, product strategy, and AI in education — with real insights .
#EdTech #LearningDesign #OnlineCourses #AIinEducation #DigitalLearning #Product

title: Galveston Cruise Guy
author: Jerry Robertson
description: The Galveston Cruise Guy podcast — real tips, real talk, and everything you need to cruise smarter on Carnival from Galveston, Texas.

title: Living Zen
author: Becoming Ordinary
description: A monthly podcast from Zen teacher, Roshi David (Jōkai)

title: Prophecy Watchers
author: Prophecy Watchers - Bible Eschatology Paranormal
description: Gary Stearman and Mondo Gonzales on Prophecy Watchers explores the intersection of biblical prophecy, current world events, archaeology, theology, and the signs of the times through the lens of Scripture. Each episode examines topics ranging from end-times prophecy and Israel's role in biblical history to emerging global events, ancient mysteries, and Christian faith, offering thoughtful analysis grounded in the Bible.Whether you're a lifelong student of prophecy or simply curious about what the Scriptures say regarding the future, Prophecy Watchers provides insightful conversations, expert guests, and practical teaching designed to encourage, educate, and strengthen your faith. Join us as we connect today's headlines with timeless biblical truths and explore the hope, purpose, and promises found throughout God's prophetic Word.

title: A Place of Prayer
author: Velda Jackson
description:
Welcome to a place of prayer, peace, and hope.

Whether you’re carrying a heavy burden, seeking direction, celebrating a blessing, or simply longing to spend time with God, you’re invited to pray here.

Each episode is a heartfelt Christian prayer centered on a specific need or topic—from healing, strength, and financial provision to families, government leaders, children, grief, forgiveness, peace, and more. These are not lessons about prayer, but opportunities to listen, pray along, and draw near to God.

No matter where you are in your faith journey, you are welcome. Quiet your heart, press play, and allow these prayers to encourage, strengthen, and remind you that you are never alone.
New prayers are added regularly so you can always find a prayer for the season you’re walking through.

When you don’t have the words to pray,
let these prayers give voice to your heart.
- Pastor Velda

Topics include: Christian prayer, guided prayer, daily prayer, prayer for healing, prayer for peace, prayer for strength, financial provision, wisdom, anxiety, grief, families, children, government leaders, hope, encouragement, faith, biblical prayer, spiritual growth, comfort, and God’s promises.

title: The Immortalised Podcast
author: Alice Barnard
description:
The Immortalised Podcast explores the lives, deaths and reported hauntings of some of history's most famous figures. Through re imagined and immersive storytelling, we will bring to life true eyewitness accounts and paranormal experiences featuring some of the world's most beloved celebrities. Each episode uncovers legends, mysteries and curious tales that just refuse to fade with time.

Afterall..


Fame Cannot Die.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: WW2 Historias
author: WW2 Historias
description:
Bienvenido a “WW2 Historias”, un espacio dedicado a descubrir poderosas historias de la Segunda Guerra Mundial, relatos poco conocidos de la historia bélica y testimonios inolvidables de valentía, sacrificio y supervivencia.

Adéntrate en un mundo donde la historia cobra vida. “WW2 Historias” es tu puerta de entrada a historias impactantes, auténticas y profundamente humanas de uno de los capítulos más importantes de la historia moderna: la Segunda Guerra Mundial.

Acompáñanos mientras exploramos los diarios, memorias y relatos personales de soldados, generales, marineros, pilotos y civiles de la Segunda Guerra Mundial que vivieron el caos de la guerra en carne propia. A través de contenido histórico cuidadosamente seleccionado, te llevamos a los frentes de batalla, los escenarios navales, las campañas militares y los cielos donde ocurrieron algunos de los momentos más dramáticos de la Segunda Guerra Mundial.

Nuestros videos dan vida a las emociones, pensamientos, luchas, sacrificios y resistencia de los hombres y mujeres que experimentaron la Segunda Guerra Mundial de primera mano. Desde historias poco contadas de la Segunda Guerra Mundial y recuerdos del campo de batalla, hasta historia militar, documentales de guerra y testimonios personales, cada episodio revela una mirada más profunda a las personas detrás de la historia.

Suscríbete ahora a “WW2 Historias” y comienza un viaje extraordinario a través de historias reales, recuerdos olvidados y poderosos relatos que marcaron toda una época, porque cada entrada de diario, cada página de memorias y cada voz de la Segunda Guerra Mundial tiene una historia que merece ser contada.

title: WW2 Tales
author: WW2 Tales
description:
Welcome to “WW2 Tales” – uncovering powerful World War II stories, untold war history, and unforgettable accounts of courage, sacrifice, and survival.

Step into a world where history comes alive. “WW2 Tales” is your gateway to gripping, authentic, and deeply human stories from one of the most important chapters in modern history: World War II.

Join us as we explore the diaries, memoirs, and personal accounts of World War II soldiers, generals, sailors, pilots, and civilians who lived through the chaos of war. Through carefully curated historical content, we take you to the battlefronts, naval theaters, military campaigns, and skies above where some of the most dramatic moments of WWII unfolded.

Our videos bring to life the emotions, thoughts, struggles, sacrifices, and resilience of the men and women who experienced the Second World War firsthand. From untold WW2 stories and battlefield memories to military history, war documentaries, and personal testimonies, every episode reveals a deeper look at the people behind the history.

Subscribe now to “WW2 Tales” and begin a remarkable journey through the real stories, forgotten memories, and powerful accounts that shaped an entire era, because every diary entry, every memoir page, and every voice from World War II has a story worth telling.

title: Teeth in a Day with Dr. Matt Annese
author: Matt Annese
description:
If you've been told your teeth are hopeless, that implants aren't for you, or that the process takes years and costs a fortune, this Podcast is where you find out what's actually possible. 


Dr. Matt Annese is a full arch implant specialist with 12 years of experience and over 2,000 cases completed at his all-digital, single-location practice in Massachusetts. 


He covers full arch dental implants, teeth-in-a-day, smile makeovers, the truth about candidacy, and why the traditional multi-office dental process is costing patients more than it should. 


New episodes weekly. No runaround. Just answers.

title: Prevent Your Heart Attack with Dr. Robert Kelly
author: Robert
description:
You work hard, sleep badly, and keep telling yourself you'll get checked out soon. 


This channel is for the stressed-out professional who knows something feels off but has not done anything about it yet.


Dr Robert Kelly is a Consultant Cardiologist and Lifestyle Medicine Physician with over 20 years of clinical experience and 10,000 patients treated. He has placed stents in people who thought they were fine. He has seen what happens when busy people wait too long.


On this Podcast channel you get the clinical facts on heart attack prevention, why stress and bad sleep are killing people faster than bad food, and the small daily steps that actually stick.

title: The American: A Journey Through European Society
description: In 'The American,' Henry James introduces us to Christopher Newman, a bright-eyed and ambitious man from California. With a newfound fortune, he sets sail for Europe, eager to explore the rich culture and old-world customs. However, Newman quickly finds himself at odds with the rigid social structures of European aristocracy. As he navigates lavish balls, intricate social dynamics, and the complexities of love, he learns that the innocence he brings from America is both a gift and a liability. This novel explores themes of identity, class, and the clash between the old and the new. Through Newman's journey, listeners will discover the challenges of adapting to unfamiliar norms while grappling with their own values. With James's keen observations and vivid characterizations, 'American' invites readers to reflect on what it means to belong. Join Christopher on his adventure as he seeks acceptance in a world that is both enchanting and unforgiving.

title: Founders Unwound
author: Alan Best
description:
The Breaking Point — How a Shot in the Pennsylvania Wilderness Set the World on Fire.

In 1754, a 22-year-old Virginia militia officer named George Washington fired a volley in the forests of Pennsylvania that a British politician later said "set the world on fire." That single event set off a chain reaction — a world war, a bankrupt British empire, a taxation crisis — that made the American Revolution not just possible, but inevitable.

This is American history told honestly. No myth. No agenda. Just the real story — messier, more complicated, and more extraordinary than anything you learned in school.

Hosts Amanda and Harry bring you inside these events from two perspectives — an American curious about her country's origins and a British historian whose nation was on the other side of it all.

Welcome to the Synergy Strong Podcast, where we share the stories behind our community and what it really means to have people in your corner.
This isn’t about workouts or programs. It’s about our “got your back” promise, ensuring you feel supported not just inside the gym, but outside our four walls too.
Each episode is meant to give you something to take with you, whether that’s a little perspective, motivation, or just the push you need for your next workout, walk, run, or bike ride.
Have someone in mind or a story worth sharing?
Email: eric@synergyfitnessteam.com

title: The Sensual Sisterhood Podcast
author: Marta Kagan & Barbara Katsnelson
description:
Most women don't need fixing.
They need aliveness.
The Sensual Sisterhood is a podcast for women who have built successful lives but no longer feel fully alive inside them.
Hosted by Marta Kagan and Barbara Katsnelson, this show explores the hidden conditioning, cultural messages, and self-abandonment patterns that leave so many women feeling numb, disconnected, exhausted, resentful, or wondering why life doesn't feel as good as they thought it would.
We talk about desire.
Pleasure.
Sex.
Relationships.
Midlife.
Aging.
Feminine power.
Body trust.
Emotional aliveness.
But this isn't really a podcast about any of those things.
It's a podcast about coming back to yourself.
Through honest conversations, personal stories, and powerful revelations, Marta and Barbara challenge everything women have been taught about what makes life meaningful, what makes a woman valuable, and what it actually takes to feel fully alive.
Because the goal isn't to become someone new.
The goal is to remember who you were before the world taught you to disconnect from yourself.
If your life looks good on paper, but something still feels missing underneath it...
Welcome back to yourself.

title: Holy Ground
author: Nicole Eveler
description:
You're highly ambitious, deeply faithful, and somehow still feel like you're not living up to what God put inside of you.
You need someone in your corner who honors all of it. The ambition. The motherhood. The faith. Without making you choose.
Holy Ground is the podcast for Christian women entrepreneurs who are done getting in their own way.
Each week, host Nicole Eveler, brand transformation strategist and founder of the Seven-Figure Steward™ Framework, brings you real conversations and solo teaching on identity, calling, and what it actually looks like to build a business and a life that reflects who God made you to be.
Expect interviews with leading Christian entrepreneurs, business and branding experts, and women navigating the intersection of faith, family, and ambition, plus solo episodes that go deep on the inner work behind the outer results.
You'll walk away with self-awareness, clear next steps, and the permission you didn't know you were waiting for.
This is your invitation to stop underutilizing what you already have and start stewarding it like the calling it is.
New episodes every week. Subscribe so you never miss one.

title: The examined life - Thoughts from Self
author: Dana Weaver
description: The Examined Life is a collection of reflections on fitness, philosophy, psychology, and the principles that shape how we live. Through lessons learned from running, strength training, and studying human nature, Dana explores what it means to think deeply, live intentionally, and play the long game.

title: THE HUMAN REPUBLIC: Inside the Most Extraordinary Machine Ever Built
author: Gregoi Rose
description:
What if your body were a nation of trillions?
What if your brain were a president constantly juggling crises, your heart a tireless transportation minister, your immune system a vast military force, and your gut home to billions of foreign citizens whose loyalty can determine your fate?
The Human Republic is a long-form storytelling podcast that explores the human body like never before.
Blending science, humor, storytelling, and vivid metaphors, each episode takes listeners inside a living civilization more complex than any country in history: their own body.
Some episodes are told through the eyes of body parts themselves. Others investigate strange biological mysteries, common myths, and surprising everyday experiences. Throughout the series, listeners gradually uncover how this incredible republic operates, adapts, survives, and sometimes rebels against its owner.
The series is designed as a continuous journey. Each episode naturally leads into the next, allowing listeners to slowly build a complete understanding of the human body while being entertained by stories, mysteries, and unforgettable characters.

title: In the Wild with Michael Bargury
author: POLDHU
description:
In the Wild is about the AI conversations worth having right now.
⠀
We're all living through the same moment: building with AI, trying to figure out what it's actually capable of, and finding where it breaks. This podcast brings together builders and breakers, researchers and practitioners - people who see things differently and challenge what we think we know about where AI is going, and what it's already doing in the wild.
⠀
Each episode hosts one of these conversations. Sometimes deep into new research, sometimes grounded in a real security problem, sometimes just a paper that raises a question no one's answered yet. You could say it's non-deterministic. Just like AI.
⠀
A Zenity Labs podcast, hosted by Michael Bargury.
⠀
Produced by POLDHU

title: Danger at the Drawbridge: A Penny Parker Mystery
description: When the groom disappears on his wedding day, the clock is ticking. Penny Parker, a sharp and adventurous young detective, dives into a web of secrets at a mysterious old estate. With the wedding at stake, she must unravel clues and confront danger lurking at every turn. This captivating tale blends suspense and friendship, as Penny navigates through hidden passages and encounters quirky characters who may hold the key to solving the mystery. Join Penny on her thrilling adventure filled with unexpected twists, as she discovers that not everything is as it seems. Perfect for young readers who love a good mystery, this story ignites imagination and encourages critical thinking. Will Penny piece together the puzzle in time to save the day? Tune in to find out!

title: Reel and Uncut Podcast | Movie Reviews
author: DremtUp Media
description:
We discuss movies, sometimes the worst & sometimes the greatest. It'll be a bit of chaos & wisdom followed by some random discussion & side quests.
If you're into hearing a few normal people casually chat about movies they love or hate this is the spot for you.

title: Beneath The Surface - David Tapp
author: Beneath The Surface - David Tapp
description: The new Beneath the Surface Podcast, produced by Power Productions, will feature special guests with compelling stories to tell. Join host David Tapp as he takes an in-depth look into the lives of individuals producing remarkable results.

title: g.a.t.o__ | Techno DJ set | Section
author: g.a.t.o__
description: Podcast by g.a.t.o__

title: Pray and Play Español - Catholic Spanish Learning for Kids
author: Celtic Casa Mama
description:
Pray and Play Español offers fun 15-20 minute Spanish language lessons with Catholic content for young learners. Each episode combines Catholic prayers and Hispanic Catholic culture with fun and comprehensible Spanish stories and grammar catered for kids 4-12. 
As your podcast host, I have a BA in Spanish and Secondary Education. In the classroom, I taught with comprehensible input strategies and compassion. I now bring my love of teaching Spanish and the Catholic faith to you - anywhere you want to listen! 
Hit “follow” to stay up to date on all new episodes released weekly!

title: Stories of Gypsy Life: Tales from the Heart of Romani Culture
description:
Immerse yourself in the vibrant world of the Romani people through Konrad Bercovici's evocative collection, *Stories of Gypsy Life*. With each tale, Bercovici draws from his own childhood, transporting listeners to a time and place where the rhythms of joy, struggle, and resilience intertwine. Experience the warmth of community, the pull of tradition, and the clash with the outside world as these stories unfold.

Meet unforgettable characters who navigate the complexities of love and loyalty against the backdrop of a society that often misunderstands them. Bercovici's rich, descriptive language paints a vivid picture of their unique customs and everyday life, allowing you to feel the pulse of their culture. Each story reveals the heart of a people who dance between freedom and confinement, illuminating the beauty and challenges of their existence.

Themes of identity, belonging, and the search for acceptance resonate throughout, inviting listeners to reflect on their own lives while fostering empathy for those who live on the margins. As you delve into these tales, you'll discover not just the stories of a distinct culture, but also a universal longing for connection and understanding. *Stories of Gypsy Life* offers an intimate glimpse into a world that is both foreign and familiar, urging us to embrace the diversity of the human experience.

title: The Clue of the Silken Ladder: A Penny Parker Mystery
description: Penny Parker, a sharp-witted teenager, dives into a web of mysteries in 'Clue of the Silken Ladder.' When a mysterious silken ladder appears in town, Penny can't resist uncovering its secrets. What is the connection to the enigmatic Japanese curio shop owner? As she investigates, Penny also seeks to expose a troupe of spiritualists who may be nothing more than clever con artists. Adding to the intrigue, a series of bizarre burglaries leaves the town on edge. Who is behind these gravity-defying crimes? With her keen instincts and a knack for problem-solving, Penny navigates twists and turns that keep listeners on the edge of their seats. This engaging adventure blends suspense, friendship, and the thrill of discovery, making it perfect for young readers and mystery lovers alike. Join Penny as she unravels clues and uncovers the truth in this captivating story that celebrates curiosity and bravery.

title: Soft Ratios Radio
author: Hoyd Breton
description: A public record of one person teaching himself to look more carefully.

title: Healthcare Without Blind Spots
author: Socially Determined
description:
Most healthcare decisions are shaped by what happens in the exam room. But patients spend 525,000+ minutes a year outside of clinical care, and what happens in those minutes matters just as much.
Healthcare Without Blind Spots brings together healthcare leaders, innovators, and practitioners who are redefining what it means to care for the whole person. Each episode explores how organizations can quantify social risk, close health equity gaps, and measure real impact by understanding the full context of patients' lives, not just their diagnoses.

title: Straight Talk On Health
author: Dave Stribling
description:
Straight Talk on Health with Little River Medical Center


Health advice without the hype. Each week, Liz sits down with medical experts from Little River Medical Center to discuss the issues that matter most—from heart health and diabetes to wellness, prevention, and healthy aging.


Real questions. Real answers. Better health.

title: THE INNER FLAME PODCAST
author: Miji Kabala
description: The Inner Flame Podcast is where spiritual awakening meets real-life transformation. Through powerful motivation, biblical truth, emotional healing, and practical mindset shifts, this show helps you keep your spiritual fire alive — even in the darkest seasons. Break generational pain. Discover your purpose. Step confidently into destiny.

title: For Anyone Listening
author: Duck LaPerriere
description:
Welcome to For Anyone Listening, an atmospheric late-night sleep podcast and quiet, low-stimulation space hosted by Duck. Tune in after dark for a softly delivered, deeply intimate curation of timeless public domain poetry, classic prose, and raw independent writing—read exactly how the words feel in the dark.
No perfection, just presence. Perfect for mindfulness, bedtime relaxation, and midnight storytelling, these readings offer honest, unvarnished human interpretations. Every episode is seamlessly layered over minimal dark ambient soundscapes, cozy fireplace crackles, and chill lo-fi beats

title: The Mechanical Heartbeat
author: Aashni Prado
description: A Beginner’s Guide to the Obsession of Luxury Watches — Shared from the perspective of a lifelong enthusiast.

title: Introducing myself: My job and studies
author: Mateo Quintero
description: In this audio, I introduce myself, describe my current job and my studies at SENA, and talk about my attitudes, beliefs and responsibilities in both my professional and academic life.

title: Connected Culture with Dr. Jamie Shapiro
author: Dr. Jamie Shapiro
description: You want an ambitious career and a life you actually want to live. You want to build teams that thrive without burning yourself out leading them. Connected Culture is the podcast for you. Each week, organizational psychologist and CEO coach Dr. Jamie Shapiro brings you research-backed conversations on leading with vitality, building healthier team cultures, and designing a career with the capacity to hold a real life around it. New episodes weekly.

title: Filmstudy - Baltimore Ravens Talk
author: Filmstudy Baltimore
description: Ken McKusick and friends join Josh Sroka from Section 336 discuss the Baltimore Ravens. Ken does an excellent job each week breaking down the Ravens game and explaining the complexities of the game in simple terms. Awesome discussions for every Ravens fan.

title: The Clock Strikes Thirteen: A Penny Parker Mystery
description: In 'Clock Strikes Thirteen,' Penny Parker, a sharp-witted teen sleuth, dives into a thrilling mystery that will keep listeners guessing. As the only daughter of Anthony Parker, the publisher of the 'Riverview Star,' Penny's life is anything but ordinary. With her keen observation skills and a knack for uncovering the truth, she tackles bizarre cases that others might shy away from. Raised to be self-sufficient and innovative, Penny balances her cheerful demeanor with a fierce determination to solve the mysteries that come her way. In this captivating story, listeners will join Penny as she navigates challenges, uncovers secrets, and learns the value of friendship and courage. Mildred A. Wirt Benson, also known for creating Nancy Drew, brings this engaging character to life, ensuring that each twist and turn keeps you on the edge of your seat. Perfect for young readers and mystery lovers alike, this audiobook promises to inspire curiosity and ignite the imagination.

title: Terminally Unfiltered
author: Cody Henthorn RN BSN
description: Terminally Unfiltered is where hospice care meets real conversation—and nothing is off limits. Hosted by healthcare professionals, we share frontline hospice stories, leadership lessons, burnout, relationships, and the humor that helps us cope. It’s the kind of talk you’d hear after a long shift—honest, unfiltered, and real. We explore what it means to care for others at the end of life while learning to care for ourselves. Expect perspective, honesty, and unexpected laughs. New episodes weekly.

title: Dogecoin Price USD Podcast
description: The Dogecoin Price USD Podcast explores the key factors that influence the value of Dogecoin beyond social media trends. Each episode discusses topics covered in the article, including overall cryptocurrency market trends, trading volume, liquidity, whale activity, real-world adoption, investor sentiment, and economic conditions. The podcast helps listeners understand the different market forces that can affect Dogecoin price USD over time.

title: Behind the Green Door: The Adventures of Penny Parker
description: Penny Parker, a determined teenage sleuth, dives headfirst into a world of mysteries that often defy explanation. Living in Riverview with her father, Anthony Parker, the publisher of the local newspaper, Penny sharpens her investigative skills as she juggles life as an amateur reporter. Her cheerful disposition and knack for observation often lead her to uncover the unexpected. In 'Behind the Green Door,' Penny faces her most challenging case yet, unraveling secrets that lie behind a mysterious door that has captivated the town's imagination. As she navigates through quirky clues and eccentric characters, Penny's tenacity and innovative thinking shine. This action-packed adventure not only showcases her detective skills but also highlights the importance of friendship and courage. Join Penny as she proves that no mystery is too big for her to tackle, and that the truth is always worth pursuing.

title: Rav Judah
author: CROTW
description: Rav Judah

title: Close Call Cases with Marcus Briggs
author: Marcus Briggs
description:
A selection of podcasts where we talk about the case studies we highlighted on our website. Close Call Cases with Marcus Briggs

Close Call Cases featuring Marcus Briggs

title: Immanuel Winnipeg Sermons
author: Will Gray
description:
Listen to sermons and teaching from Immanuel Winnipeg Anglican Church. 
Immanuel Winnipeg is a biblically faithful, Gospel sharing Anglican Church plant in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada. We exist to help people in all ages and stages of life know Jesus, grow in Jesus, and share Jesus with others. 
Website: www.immanuelwinnipeg.org
Instagram: www.instagram.com/immanuel.winnipeg/

title: The Retreat Machine
author: Retreat Machine
description:
Hosted by Colet and Nick, the Retreat Machine team, this show goes behind the surface of modern entrepreneurship, retreats, and community to explore the systems most people never see, but everyone is quietly building on.
This is not another marketing podcast about doing more, posting more, or chasing the next strategy. It is about something quieter and far more powerful, the structure underneath it all.
Each episode breaks down how audiences become communities, how content becomes continuity, and how retreat businesses, brands, and personal ecosystems are really built to sustain over time, not just perform in the moment.
Through honest conversation, lived experience, and thoughtful analysis, Colet and Nick explore the shift from visibility to longevity, from attention to trust, and from content creation to system creation.
If you are building a retreat, a brand, a podcast, or anything meant to last beyond a launch cycle, this is your inside look at how the machine really works.
Welcome to The Retreat Machine, the podcast helping retreat leaders build what lasts.

title: The Sacred Green
author: Sacred Green
description: Exploring the connection between the earth we walk on and the beliefs we hold. Join me for local stories, philosophy, religion, and the truly human.

title: Stuck at Zero: Why Your Online Business Isn't Making Any Money + What To Do About It
author: Gillian Perkins
description:
Your business isn't making the money you want it to make. Maybe it's been months. Maybe it's been years. And by now you've probably started asking yourself some hard questions: Is my offer good enough? Am I just bad at sales? Is my niche too crowded? Am I even cut out for this?

Stick with me, because those probably aren't the real problem.

I'm Gillian Perkins. I've run my online business for almost 10 years (the last eight or nine profitable), grown an audience of nearly a million followers, and brought in over $2.5 million along the way. I've also spent the last seven years working with over 3,000 clients and customers, helping them build businesses that actually make money. So I've seen this problem from both sides - lived it, and helped fix it for others.

In this series, I'm walking you through exactly why your business is stuck at zero and exactly what to do about it:

Episode 1: Why your offer, your messaging, and your sales funnel probably aren't the real problem - and what's actually going on
Episode 2: The real reason your business isn't making sales (it's probably not what you think)
Episode 3: The common "fixes" people try, and why they don't actually solve the problem
Episode 4: The simple formula behind consistent, predictable revenue
Episode 5: What to prioritize to start making real money - and how to scale once you do

If your business has been stuck at zero, it's time to figure out why... and what you can do to finally start making money.

title: Body Intelligence
author: Maurice Hathaway
description:
Body Intelligence explores the science of how your body and mind communicate to shape health, performance, and longevity. Each episode breaks down complex topics in neuroscience, metabolism, fitness, psychology, and biology into practical insights you can use in daily life.

From brain chemistry and stress regulation to nutrition, movement, and recovery, this podcast helps you understand how your body truly works—and how to work with it instead of against it.

The goal is simple: upgrade your awareness, optimize your biology, and build a healthier, more capable version of yourself.

title: The Board Game Secret Show
author: neongorillasgames
description: shhhh....don't tell anyone but we talk to interesting people in the board game hobby.

title: Ash & Honey: The Root and The Road
author: Alexandria Quinn Love
description:
Before the internet told you what to eat, what to take, and what to fear—somebody’s grandmother already knew. And she learned it from her grandmother.

Ash & Honey: The Root and The Road is a history podcast about healing. Not wellness. Not optimization. Healing—the kind with a lineage, a name, and a reason that predates the algorithm selling it back to you.

Hosted by Alexandria Quinn Love, M.A.—historian, formulator, and founder of Ash & Honey Botanique—this show digs into the pre-industrial record: Greek Humorism, Ayurveda, Celtic and Pictish traditions, the raw economics of medieval medicine, and the specific plants that kept people alive before the supplement aisle existed. Then, we turn that same lens on what’s circulating on your feed. Not to tear it down, but to find out where the real thing went—and what got stripped out between the source and the shelf.

The body is not separate from the mind. The mind is not separate from the emotion. The emotion is not separate from the season.

Some episodes are history. Some are honest. Some are both. Some are just real life crossing the microphone, because an integrated life doesn't perform for production value.

Take everything with a grain of salt. Including this.

The road was always there. Someone always walked it. Now—so do you.

title: Abraham Hicks’s Podcast
author: Abraham Hicks
description:
Millions of people around the world use the techniques of Abraham Hicks to create happier, more fulfilling lives. This channel is dedicated to educating and sharing Abraham’s teachings, offering practical methods to help you align with your desires, improve your well-being, and consciously shape the life you want. Each video provides educational value recognized and appreciated by viewers worldwide, with tools and insights to guide you toward joy, abundance, and personal empowerment.

🚨 All Abraham-Hicks audio materials are copyrighted by Esther Hicks and we are sharing these following the USE OF COPYRIGHTED MATERIAL GUIDELINES *** All audio files on this channel have been purchased by us and are shared in accordance with Esther Hicks' guidelines.

title: The Whispering Eye: A Black Hood Novel
description: In a city swallowed by shadows, the Black Hood finds himself in a deadly game of survival. Framed for robbery and murder, he must navigate the treacherous streets while evading the relentless police and the ruthless underworld. At the center of this chaos is Barbara Sutton, the woman whose love he desperately seeks, but who also sees him as a criminal. With danger lurking at every corner, the Black Hood faces the infamous Eye, a master fiend whose grip on the city is tightening. Can he clear his name and win back Barbara's trust, or will he fall victim to the very forces that seek to destroy him? Dive into this gripping detective fiction where every choice could lead to redemption or ruin. Experience a tale filled with suspense, betrayal, and a quest for love in a world where nothing is as it seems.

title: The Biodiversity Hotspot
author: Rey
description: Rey Malcolm talks to people all around the globe about nature and how we can help do our part

title: Your Best Friends in Business
author: Stacie & Madie | Pod People
description:
What happens when two best friends who’ve built multiple businesses together decide to stop keeping the good stuff to themselves?

Join co-founders Stacie Staub and Madie Linder — the team behind West and Main Homes and a growing roster of businesses they’ve built from scratch — for the kind of conversations most founders only have behind closed doors. Each episode opens with a real-time business update (the wins, the chaos, the things that are very much still in progress), then brings in a founder who’s building for the long game for an honest, unfiltered conversation about the decisions that actually shaped their company.

Part playbook, part therapy session, and part best-friend gab session; Your Best Friends in Business blends candid interviews, opinionated debates about what works and what definitely doesn’t, and the kind of practical takeaways that only come from people who are still in it.

No TED Talk polish. No highlight reel. Just real founders, real decisions, and real talk.

Come for the strategies. Stay for the honesty. Leave with a reminder that there’s no perfect way to build a business so just build it!

title: Nef's Take
author: Nefreteri
description:
Nef's Take is a cultural audit. Every episode traces a modern problem — political, social, psychological — back to where it started, shows how it morphed on the way down, and what it's actually costing you now.  
If you're tired of content that picks a tribe and calls it analysis, but you actually want to understand what's happening and why — this is the alternative.  No team. No contempt. No side to defend.

title: The Shameless Romantic
author: Kat Wilhoit
description: It's time to get a little shameless and expand your mind! This is a safe space to discuss all things love, sex, relationships and the human experience. Whether you're new to any of these topics or an experienced player of the respected game - you're bound to learn something new on our weekly podcast. Join in on the juicy fun, kinky know-how and sexier education! Music credit: Acapulco Clementine

title: The Thousandth Woman: A Love Tested by Murder
description: In a gripping tale set against the backdrop of late 19th-century London, E.W. Hornung introduces us to a remarkable woman who defies societal expectations. The Thousandth Woman centers on a fierce and loyal heroine who stands by her man when he is wrongfully accused of murder. As the tension rises, she navigates a web of intrigue, facing societal pressures and personal dilemmas. This detective fiction novel not only captivates with its thrilling plot but also offers a fresh perspective on female agency during a transformative time in history. Hornung's storytelling shines a light on the complexities of love and loyalty, making this an engaging listen for those who appreciate strong characters and suspenseful narratives. Discover how one woman's unwavering support can challenge the norms of her era, while uncovering the truth behind the accusations. Join us on this journey through the streets of London, where every twist and turn keeps you guessing until the very end.

title: The Black Box: A Quest for Justice
description: When Sanford Quest, the world's leading criminologist, takes on a high-profile case, he has no idea of the deadly game that awaits him. The brutal murder of Lord Ashleigh's daughter sends shockwaves through society, and Quest is determined to bring the killer to justice. As he delves deeper into the investigation, he uncovers a web of deception and danger that leads him to a shadowy master criminal. With every clue he uncovers, the stakes rise, and Quest finds himself in a life-and-death battle against an unseen adversary who will stop at nothing to protect their secrets. 'Black Box' is a gripping tale of wit, intrigue, and relentless pursuit that will keep you on the edge of your seat. Join Quest as he navigates the twists and turns of this thrilling mystery, filled with unexpected revelations and perilous encounters.

title: Holding it together-ish
author: Veronica
description:
A podcast by a 42-year-old mum of two, navigating a blended family, a demanding full-time job, and the joys (and confusion) of perimenopause.
I’ve spent years listening to podcasts—now I’m finally giving it a go myself. No grand plan, no polished answers… just real conversations while I figure things out.
If you’re also questioning everything and muddling through, you’re in good company.

title: LOSERS
author: Konner
description:
Just two comedians in New York trying to navigate the modern world while taking countless L's. How original. 


Hosted by Bo Bigelow and Konner Kienzle.

title: Quit Your Worrying! Finding Freedom from Anxiety
description: Worry can feel like a constant companion, but it doesn’t have to be. In 'Quit Your Worrying!', George Wharton James shares practical strategies that helped him break free from the grip of anxiety. This audiobook offers relatable insights into the nature of worry and how it affects our daily lives. James emphasizes that there isn't a one-size-fits-all solution, but he provides valuable tools that anyone can adapt to their own situation. Through personal anecdotes and straightforward advice, listeners will discover how to cultivate a sense of serenity and reclaim their peace of mind. Each chapter invites you to reflect on your own worries, explore new perspectives, and take actionable steps toward a more relaxed life. If you’re ready to let go of anxiety and embrace a calmer mindset, this audiobook is your guide to a more fulfilling and worry-free existence.

title: The Country Beyond: Wilderness Romance
description: In the heart of the Northwest Canadian wilderness, 'Jolly' Roger McKay is an outcast on the run. His life takes a thrilling turn when he meets Nada, a captivating woman who ignites a spark of hope amid his chaos. Together, they navigate the challenges of the wild, facing dangers that test their courage and commitment. Adding depth to their journey is Peter, a loyal mixed-breed dog whose bond with Roger and Nada is as strong as any human connection. As they search for 'The Country Beyond,' they encounter breathtaking landscapes, fierce challenges, and the complexities of love in a rugged setting. This tale of action, adventure, and romance explores the power of loyalty and the quest for belonging in a world that feels both vast and isolating. Join Roger and Nada as they discover not only the wilderness but also the strength within themselves and each other.

title: Virtual Grip Podcast
author: Virtual Grip
description: Welcome to the Virtual Grip podcast, where reality is optional and gaming is limitless. Join Paul from the Virtual Grip and Dr Oculus as they dive into the immersive worlds of VR and the on-the-go thrill of handheld gaming. From cutting-edge tech to hidden gems, they’ve got your gaming fix covered—anytime, anywhere. Plug in, power up, and get a grip on the future of play.

title: Wyoming Wildlife Podcast
author: Wyoming Game and Fish Department
description: The Wyoming Wildlife Podcast is an official Wyoming Game and Fish Department Podcast covering all things wildlife. We'll speak with wildlife experts across the state to share agency news and information about wildlife and conservation projects in Wyoming. New episodes are released the first Wednesday of every month at 11:00 a.m. MT.

title: Why Most Actors Never Get Signed and how to fix it.
author: crystalshipartists
description: Most actors never get signed. Not because they’re not talented, but because they don’t understand how they’re being evaluated.

author: Nothing Is Over Podcast
description: Entertainment show hosted by @BobKellyJnr

title: NuggTalk™
author: planetnuggstudios
description:
Hosts:
Wally Bonilla — Nuggtalk Co-Host
Jimmy McCune — Nuggtalk Co-Host
Visit Planet Nugg: PlanetNugg.com
Subscribe for more Nuggtalk episodes featuring cannabis culture, brand stories, product education, New York’s legal cannabis scene, and the people shaping the industry.
Must be 21 or older. Please consume responsibly and follow all New York State cannabis laws.

title: The Authority Gap: Companion Podcast
author: Derek Little
description: AI-hosted debates and deep dives from The Authority Gap, exploring why consulting firms lose deals they should win — and how authority helps buyers understand, trust, and choose them.

title: Fact or Fiction
author: Squee's House of Pizza
description: A Premodern Magic: The Gathering podcast from the Squee's House of Pizza crew. Tournament reports, deck deep dives, and honest takes on the format, for the people already brewing in it.

title: Won & Done
author: Fantasy Sports Advice Network
description:
Hosted by Jay Heinrich and Mike Holland, Won & Done is your fast break of college basketball DFS and betting information, live every week throughout the CBB season. Jay and Mike break down the biggest slates on the calendar — cash game plays, GPP leverage, player prop angles, and betting lines — giving you everything you need to build smarter lineups and sharper bets.


From November tip-off through Championship Week, Selection Sunday, and March Madness, the guys bring you year-round ncaa basketball coverage. Coaching interviews, transfer portal, matchup breakdowns, value plays, injury and rotation notes, and plenty of debate over who’s chalk and who’s a fade. Whether you’re grinding cash games, chasing a GPP, or just looking for an edge on tonight’s card, Won & Done has you covered.


New episodes every week, with multiple shows per week during the college basketball season. Follow along on X @wonanddonecbb for daily slate previews and updates.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Healing Through Love
author: Healing Through Love
description:
Healing Through Love – The Podcast


 Empowering survivors. Elevating voices. Inspiring change.


Welcome to Healing Through Love, a safe space where survivors of domestic and family violence are seen, heard, and supported. Hosted by Sharlene Lynch and Rose Davidson, co-founders of the Healing Through Love movement, this podcast shares heartfelt conversations with survivors, advocates, and professionals who are creating change, breaking cycles, and offering hope.


Each episode is a journey of courage, healing, and empowerment – filled with real stories, practical tools, and soul-nourishing support. Whether you're a survivor reclaiming your voice, a practitioner offering healing, or an ally wanting to make a difference, this podcast is for you.


Together, we shine a light on the path from pain to purpose.


 Together, we heal – through love.


🎧 New episodes released regularly
 💜 Learn more at healingthroughlove.com.au

title: CBS Radio Mystery Theater - 1978
description:
A new episode daily. CBS Radio Mystery Theater was a noteworthy attempt to revive in American radio dramas like Inner Sanctum (1941-1952) and Suspense (1942-1962). Radio dramas were widely considered "dead" 12 years prior to this series. CBS Radio Mystery Theater, or simply Mystery Theater, was created by Inner Sanctum creator Himan Brown and ran on CBS from 1974-1982. The show, much like older radio dramas, was introduced by a host (in this case E.G. Marshall), who steers us through the creaking door to start the episode. Many voices from the golden age of radio were featured, including Richard Widmark, Bret Morrison and Agnes Moorehead.

Find all our OTR radio stations and podcasts at theaterofthemind-otr.com - Audio Credit: The Old Time Radio Researchers Group. - All Podcasts @ Spreaker | Apple | YouTube |

CBS Radio Mystery Theater - Broadcast & Reception
  • Aired every night at midnight in most markets, creating a "late-night spooky radio" vibe.
  • One of the last successful network radio drama series before the format faded.
  • Gained a cult following and is still beloved by old-time radio (OTR) fans today.     
Hosts & Narration
  • E.G. Marshall (1974–1982, primary host) – Known for his deep, authoritative voice, setting a chilling tone.
  • Tammy Grimes (1974–1977, occasional alternate host) – Provided a more whimsical, sometimes eerie presence.
  • The host would introduce each episode with a dramatic monologue, often breaking the fourth wall to engage listeners.

title: The Mush Room 🍄
author: John Staniszewski
description:
Johnny Staniszewski should not be alive. Twenty Norcos a day, two Adderall, a Soma to sleep, and a grief he had no language for. Then four grams of mushrooms sitting in his closet for six months gave him a come-to-God moment that ended two years of opiate dependency overnight.
The Mush Room is what came after.
Part confession, part field report, part manifesto, this show covers the full spectrum of what mushrooms can do: healing addiction and chronic illness, rewiring habits from the ground up, rebuilding the food system, and exposing an entire supplement industry built on mycelium on grain and zero accountability.
Johnny has grown half a million pounds of mushrooms, put them in 150 grocery stores and 50 restaurants, and shipped thousands of pounds of marinara to Chicago public schools. He has sat in ceremony every month for a year and a half and facilitated psilocybin sessions across the country. He built Forgotten Supplements because he got tired of watching people trying to heal get sold garbage.
The mission is food and psilocybin rehabilitation centers all over the world. Ten million Fungitarians is where it starts.
