This series, which aired during the golden age of radio, features the brilliant detective Sherlock Holmes and his loyal companion Dr. John Watson as they solve intricate mysteries and unravel complex cases.

📻 Each episode presents a new adventure filled with suspense, deduction, and the signature wit of Holmes. Listeners can expect thrilling tales of crime, clever plot twists, and the atmospheric charm of vintage radio storytelling. The series captures the essence of detective fiction, making it a staple for fans of the genre.

🎭 As a notable entry in the Old Time Radio canon, Sherlock Holmes - John Stanley appeals to collectors and enthusiasts of classic detective stories. Its enduring popularity is a testament to the timeless nature of Holmes' adventures and the captivating performances of the cast.

🔎 This vintage radio series remains a significant part of the detective genre, offering full-length broadcasts that continue to engage audiences with their clever narratives and iconic characters.

title: HumorUs — Funny Short Stories
author: Don McDonald
description:
Comedy is the oldest trick in the book (or the short story), and HumorUs goes digging for the funniest classic tale with a few new ones sprinkled in. Each episode is one short story, narrated with care and a raised eyebrow. Bureaucratic guinea pigs that simply will not stop multiplying, courtesy of Ellis Parker Butler. A backwoods divorce that doubles right back into a wedding, by way of O. Henry. A shipwreck that strands exactly the wrong man, from the dry wit of W.W. Jacobs. A committee so devoted to oversight it forms a committee to oversee itself. Some of these tales are a hundred years old. Some are brand new. All of them prove the same stubborn little fact, that a good laugh never goes out of print. So pour something, settle in, and let the great humorists, famous and forgotten alike, humor us. A comedy fiction podcast from Short Storyverses.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Prelim Brief
author: Graham Diedrich
description: The Prelim Brief distills the foundational books, landmark papers, and core debates that define political science. Each episode breaks down a major work in American politics.

title: BGRE Podcast
author: Brandon Greenplate
description:
The Brandon Greenplate Real Estate Podcast is where real estate, entrepreneurship, investing, and business come together.


Hosted by Brandon Greenplate, each episode features conversations with entrepreneurs, investors, industry leaders, and top performers who are building wealth, creating opportunity, and challenging conventional thinking. From real estate investing and business growth to leadership, mindset, and the strategies behind long-term success, every conversation is designed to deliver practical insights you can apply to your own journey.


Whether you're a real estate professional, investor, entrepreneur, or simply someone looking to think bigger, this podcast is built to help you create more opportunities, build lasting wealth, and leave a meaningful legacy.


Sell More. Own More. Live More.

title: Killer Konversations With Steven Fisher
author: Rhythm House
description:
Killer Konversations

What drives ordinary people to do extraordinary things? Why are we fascinated by evil, the supernatural, history, and the mysteries of human behavior?

Hosted by bestselling psychological thriller author Steven Fisher, Killer Konversations explores the stories behind the stories. Each episode features fascinating conversations with authors, criminologists, historians, psychologists, paranormal experts, museum curators, investigators, and unique personalities who challenge the way we think about human nature.

From serial killers and criminal psychology to haunted history, folklore, travel, and the enduring battle between good and evil, every conversation is driven by curiosity and one central question:

Why do people do what they do?

If you’re captivated by true crime, psychological thrillers, history, or the darker corners of the human experience, Killer Konversations is a podcast you won’t want to miss.

title: Matthew Slade, Private Investigator – Old Time Radio Detective (OTR)
description:
🎧 Matthew Slade, Private Investigator is a classic Old Time Radio detective drama that immerses listeners in the gritty world of crime and investigation. Set against the backdrop of urban crime scenes, this series showcases the trials and tribulations of a hardboiled private investigator navigating through a labyrinth of mysteries and deceit. With its roots in the golden age of radio, this series captures the essence of 1940s and 1950s detective storytelling.

📻 Each episode features recurring themes of suspense, intrigue, and the relentless pursuit of justice. Listeners can expect to encounter a variety of characters, from cunning criminals to desperate clients, all woven into compelling narratives that highlight the complexities of crime-solving. The series is structured around episodic mysteries, often culminating in unexpected resolutions that keep audiences engaged.

🎭 For collectors and enthusiasts of vintage radio, Matthew Slade, Private Investigator stands as a notable entry in the detective genre. Its compelling storylines and classic radio format resonate with fans of similar shows such as Dragnet and Gang Busters. This series remains a cherished part of Old Time Radio history, offering full-length broadcasts that capture the essence of detective drama in its prime.

🔎 Discover the world of classic detective radio with Matthew Slade, Private Investigator, and enjoy a collection of vintage broadcasts that exemplify the art of storytelling in Old Time Radio.

title: Matinee Theater – Old Time Radio Drama (OTR)
description:
🎧 Matinee Theater is a classic Old Time Radio drama series that transports listeners to the golden age of radio storytelling. This vintage collection features a variety of dramatic narratives, showcasing the talents of skilled performers and captivating scripts that engage the imagination. Each episode unfolds a unique tale, often exploring themes of love, conflict, and the human experience, all set against the backdrop of the mid-20th century.

📻 The series is characterized by its anthology format, where each episode presents a standalone story, allowing for a diverse range of characters and settings. Expect to encounter everything from heartfelt romances to intense conflicts, all delivered with the dramatic flair that defines the genre. Listeners can anticipate a blend of emotional depth and engaging storytelling, typical of the era's radio dramas.

🎭 Matinee Theater remains a notable series among OTR enthusiasts, appealing to collectors who appreciate the artistry of classic radio performances. Fans of vintage radio dramas and those interested in the evolution of storytelling will find this series a valuable addition to their collections. The series stands as a testament to the rich history of Old Time Radio, preserving the art of drama through full-length broadcasts and preserved episodes.

🔔 Tune in for more episodes of Matinee Theater, classic Old Time Radio dramas, and curated selections from the Heritage Radio Vault.

title: Mark Trail – Old Time Radio Adventure (OTR)
description:
🎧 Mark Trail is a classic Old Time Radio adventure series that transports listeners to the great outdoors, where nature and wildlife take center stage. This thrilling drama, rooted in the golden age of radio, features the titular character, Mark Trail, a rugged outdoorsman and wildlife photographer, who embarks on various escapades that highlight the beauty and dangers of nature.

📻 Each episode presents a new challenge, often involving conservation efforts, wildlife encounters, and the protection of natural habitats. Listeners can expect a blend of adventure, environmental themes, and the spirit of exploration as Mark navigates through forests, rivers, and mountains, often confronting villains who threaten the wilderness.

🎭 Collectors and enthusiasts of vintage radio will appreciate the engaging storytelling and the series' commitment to promoting nature conservation. Mark Trail remains a notable entry in the adventure genre, appealing to fans of outdoor tales and heroic narratives.

🔎 This series is a must-listen for those interested in classic adventure dramas, wildlife stories, and the enduring legacy of Old Time Radio broadcasts.

title: The Revival Show
author: Drake Robert & Noah Doss
description: The Revival Show is the podcast for boat owners, detailers, and anyone building something in the marine industry — hosted by Drake, founder of Revival Marine Care, and Noah, Revival’s Sales Director. Real conversations about marine care, business, and life, every week on YouTube and all major podcast platforms.

title: The Kylen and Kenda Show
author: Riley Byrne
description: The Kylen and Kenda Show is an unfiltered, funny, and surprisingly tender conversation between a mentor and the mentee she refuses to give up on. Kenda and Kylen dig into culture, technology, relationships, mental health, and what it actually takes to grow up in a world that keeps us lonely and online.

title: Next Step Conversations
author: Becca Pitts
description: Real conversations about the moves that matter most. Whether you're buying your first home, selling the one you've lived in for 30 years, or helping a parent figure out what comes next, Becca Pitts sits down with the people who make every step easier: lenders, inspectors, contractors, financial planners, elder law attorneys, in-home care providers, aging-in-place specialists, and more. Part real estate education, part senior transition guide, all honest talk about your next step.

title: hems&ems
author: Shalom Philou Media Inc
description: A friendly Q&A podcast where youth and young adult church leaders answer real questions from their group and talk honestly about faith in today’s world.

title: The Hand in the Dark: A Country House Mystery
description: In the autumn of 1918, an ancient English country house becomes the backdrop for a chilling mystery. The War to End All Wars rages on in Europe, but in this seemingly peaceful setting, a bored young wife throws a party for her London friends. Among the guests are war workers, servicemen, and a renowned explorer known for his expertise in jewels. The evening takes a dark turn when, just hours later, the hostess is found dead from a gunshot wound. An heirloom pearl necklace has vanished, and Scotland Yard is hot on the trail—yet they may be targeting the wrong suspect. As secrets unravel and tension builds, can the true story behind the murder be uncovered before it's too late? Join the hunt for clues in this captivating tale of intrigue, betrayal, and the quest for justice. "Hand in the Dark" invites listeners into a world where every character holds a piece of the puzzle and nothing is quite as it seems.

title: The Breach Desk
author: Carlos Alves
description:
Every week, Carlos Cardal Alves, CISO and founder of CRS Consulting, takes the week's most significant breach, strips it down to the mechanism that made it possible, maps it to the regulatory exposure, and hands you one action to take before the next one lands.
No jargon for its own sake. No vendor pitches. Just the breach and the lessons thereof.

title: The Castle of Otranto
description:
Step into the haunting world of The Castle of Otranto, the groundbreaking Gothic novel by Horace Walpole that inspired generations of supernatural fiction. Set within an ancient castle filled with dark secrets, mysterious prophecies, hidden passages, and unexplained events, this unforgettable story follows the ambitious Prince Manfred as fate begins to unravel his family's legacy. As ghostly apparitions, ominous warnings, and impossible phenomena surround the castle, every chapter builds suspense and mystery.

Blending medieval romance, psychological tension, and chilling Gothic atmosphere, The Castle of Otranto remains one of the most influential classics in literary history. Its unforgettable characters and eerie setting continue to captivate listeners more than 250 years after its publication.

Enjoy this timeless public-domain audiobook in high-quality audio and discover the novel that gave birth to Gothic fiction. Perfect for fans of haunted castles, supernatural mysteries, historical horror, and classic literature. Summary by DreamAudiobooks

title: 2 Scrappy Chicks
author: Ginger & Marie
description:
Welcome to 2 Scrappy Chicks – the podcast where two fearless women dive into real talk about life, business, and everything in between.

Join your hosts, Ginger and Marie as they share their unfiltered perspectives on chasing dreams, overcoming obstacles, and having the guts to build something amazing from scratch. Whether you're an entrepreneur, hustler, or just someone who loves a good story, 2 Scrappy Chicks is your new go-to for laughs, inspiration, and honest conversations.

Each week, expect a mix of bold advice, personal journeys, and a few laughs along the way. It's like hanging out with your best friends, but with a dose of grit, determination, and a sprinkle of humor. Grab your headphones – it's time to get scrappy!

title: Glen Pearson Journeys Podcast
author: Glen
description:
Glen Pearson has spent his entire adult life in pursuit of public service, democracy, and the primacy of citizenship. But those words can sound abstract until you see what they really mean over the course of a human life. They mean showing up when others turn away. They mean believing that communities are not held together by systems alone, but by moral commitments — by ordinary people who decide, day after day, that the lives around them matter.


Over decades of work in politics, humanitarian service, community leadership, and writing, Pearson has come to understand something that modern society often forgets: a healthy democracy is not primarily built in legislatures, media studios, corporate towers, or in algorithms. It is built in the quiet habits of citizenship. It is built whenever people choose responsibility over cynicism, contribution over withdrawal, and neighbourliness over isolation.


For much of his life, he has tried to give language to those truths. Through more than seventy books, countless articles, podcasts, and his long-standing column in the London Free Press, Glen has chronicled not merely political events, but the deeper moral and social currents moving beneath them. His work consistently returns to the same central question: how do human beings remain connected to one another in an age that so often pulls them apart?


That question has become especially urgent in our time. We live amid extraordinary technological advancement and unprecedented material convenience, yet many people move through life carrying a quiet loneliness. Institutions that once formed identity and belonging have weakened. Public life has grown harsher, more performative, more tribal. Too often citizenship itself has been reduced to outrage, branding, or consumption, as though democracy were simply a marketplace of grievances rather than a shared moral project.

Pearson’s life and writing offer another vision.


He argues that meaning is not found in endless self-preoccupation, but in service. That purpose emerges when people dedicate themselves to something larger than personal advancement. That communities flourish when citizens stop asking only what they are owed and begin asking what they are called to give.

Again and again, his work points toward the small but transformative acts that renew societies: feeding neighbours, mentoring children, protecting institutions, listening across differences, creating spaces where dignity can survive. He believes the future will not ultimately be saved by ideology or spectacle, but by the patient rebuilding of trust between people who learn once more how to see one another as fellow travellers rather than enemies.  Thus the title of this site – Journeys.


At the heart of Pearson’s message is a profound faith in human possibility. Not a naïve optimism that denies suffering or division, but a deeper conviction that people are capable of moral courage when summoned toward a worthy purpose. He reminds us that citizenship is not merely a legal status. It is a moral vocation. Democracy survives only when enough people choose to carry one another through difficult times.

In an age searching desperately for meaning, Glen Pearson invites us back to the enduring truths that make both lives and nations worth building: service, decency, sacrifice, community, and love.

title: Dawn Della Rose's podcast
author: Dawn Della Rose
description: Small towns, deep roots, and small-time religion meant to replace legends. In these spaces, appearance is everything and information is a currency—hoarded to protect the powerful and weaponized to maintain total control. This podcast maps the anatomy of family secrets, the weight of generational skeletons, and the institutional patterns that govern our quietest corners. Some stories are lived; some are observed. All of them are true.

title: Revenge! Tales of Crime and Intrigue
description: In 'Revenge!', Robert Barr captivates listeners with a gripping collection of 20 short stories that delve into the dark side of human nature. Each tale weaves a narrative centered on the theme of revenge, where characters face moral dilemmas and unexpected twists. From cunning detectives to vengeful adversaries, Barr's masterful storytelling draws you into a world where justice is often served in surprising ways. You’ll find echoes of Alfred Hitchcock’s thrilling style throughout these narratives, adding a layer of intrigue that keeps you on the edge of your seat. As you navigate through these tales, expect the unexpected and let your imagination explore the fine line between retribution and justice. Perfect for fans of detective fiction and thrillers, this audiobook promises a captivating listening experience that will resonate long after the last story ends.

title: The Man in Lower Ten: A Detective Mystery
description: On a routine trip to Pittsburgh, Lawrence Blakeley faces an unexpected twist when he discovers his bank notes and clothes have vanished from his train berth. Tasked with verifying the authenticity of forged notes, Blakeley’s journey quickly spirals into a gripping adventure. As he navigates the mystery of the missing items, he encounters deception, danger, and a cast of intriguing characters. With each twist and turn, the stakes rise, and Blakeley must use his wits to uncover the truth behind this bizarre heist. Will he solve the mystery before it’s too late? Dive into this classic detective tale filled with suspense and clever plotting that keeps you guessing until the last moment. Mary Roberts Rinehart, known as the 'American Agatha Christie,' masterfully blends humor and intrigue, making this audiobook a captivating listen for fans of detective fiction.

title: Chaos Conjure
author: Dottie the Psychic
description: In the mountains of Appalachia, two dynamic women, Dottie the Psychic and Jacinta (Jai) of Autumn Sky LLC, are redefining what it means to walk a spiritual path in the modern world. Rooted in the rich, natural landscape of West Virginia, both women embrace their unique practices while fostering connection and empowerment within their communities.

title: Pixels, Place and Purpose
author: David Title, Bravo Media, Manny Almagro, Marks - A Propelis Company
description: Pixels, Places & Purpose examines how brands get created, experienced, and proven in an AI-driven world. Hosts Manolo Almagro - Chief Innovation Officer at Marks, A Propelis Company x David Title - Partner at Bravo Media dig into the questions reshaping the industry: what AI can actually deliver, how creative gets produced at scale, where brand experience is headed and how to prove it worked. Expect real data over vendor hype and honest takes built for industry execs who'd rather build what works than chase what's loud—from two people doing the work, not just talking about it.

title: The Falesha Raquel Show
author: Falesha Raquel
description:
The Falesha Raquel Show, hosted by serial entrepreneur, speaker and author Falesha Raquel, alongside finance expert and mortgage broker co-host Mark McLaughlin,  brings bold and insightful conversations on real estate,  entrepreneurship, personal development, life and more.

Streaming every other Wednesday at 3:00 PM EST, each episode features dynamic discussions, practical strategies, real-world experiences, and meaningful conversations. 

The show is created for those who are ready to think bigger, move with purpose, and build success that impacts generations.


Follow us on IG: https://www.instagram.com/thefalesharaquelshow_/

Follow Falesha Raquel: https://www.instagram.com/falesha_raquel/

Follow Mark McLaughlin: https://www.instagram.com/markmclaughlin_13/

title: CHRIST ABOVE ALL - A Study in Colossians
author: Pastor Robert Griffith
description: A 12 part teaching series on Paul's letter to the Colossians

title: Becoming Unoffendable - Faith Chat
author: Raglan Community Media
description:
Taking Becoming Unoffendable blogs deeper as we explore them from a faith perspective.Interviews from the Faith Matters show, including 
Becoming Unoffendable with Denise J Gardener
Mariage Matters with Jax and Umberto Benucci
Pastor's Corner with Roger Peart
Plus stories to inspire and encourage as we journey through this thing called life.

Taken from the Faith Matters show broadcast on Raglan Community Radio on Fridays at midday.

title: The Cycle Ends With Us
author: Jenni Mottmiller
description: Have you ever felt like you have done everything you were told growing up? Go to school, get the job, be loyal and life would be good? Feel like that didn't happen? Join me every week as we break the cycles and build the life you know you deserve!

title: JustFU
author: The OG’s
description:
JustFU, short for Just For Us, is a comedy and storytelling podcast from the North Carolina coast where three old friends, Peter Burrows, Steve Clarken, and Mark Dirks, get together to do what they've been doing for decades: swapping stories, sharing opinions, and laughing at life's absurdities.

From parenting adult children and surviving marriage to golf, travel, food,
friendships, and everyday frustrations, the guys tackle relatable topics with Southern humor, honesty, and plenty of friendly ribbing. Recorded in their garage studio near the North Carolina coast, JustFU offers listeners a front- row seat to the kind of conversations that usually happen when old friends gather around with nowhere special to be and nowhere important to go.

Whether you're a parent, golfer, traveler, retiree, empty nester, young professional, or simply someone who appreciates authentic storytelling and comedy, you'll find plenty to laugh about.

And if not? Well... JustFU.

title: Active Life Blueprint
author: razuahmedpodcastpromoter
description: Active Life Blueprint is a health and fitness podcast designed to help you create a stronger, healthier, and more active lifestyle. Each episode shares practical fitness tips, nutrition advice, wellness strategies, and motivation to build lasting habits that improve both physical strength and mental well-being every day.

title: Tony and William Tutoring Show (TAWT)
author: Tony Richmond
description: Two experienced tutors explore the tutoring landscape from their unique points of view. Meet Tony, a Science and Maths tutor at both GCSE and A-level. Meet William, an A-level Economics teacher and tutor. Both met in the early noughties at an international school in the south of China before qualifying as secondary school teachers and have remained friends ever since. Why not collaborate in a podcast to explore the educational experience, they thought. So here it is!

title: A.T.rail Of History
author: Appalachian Trail Museum
description: A.T.rail of History tells the stories of the Appalachian Trail. It will carry out the mission of the A.T. Museum to tell the story of the A.T., by telling the stories of the founding, construction, preservation, maintenance, protection, and enjoyment of the Trail since its creation.

title: Widow's Permission Slips with Gladys Jean Ullstam
author: Gladys Jean Ullstam
description:
Nobody hands you a rulebook when your person dies. But somehow, everybody seems to have an opinion about how you should be grieving — how long it should take, how sad you're allowed to be, when you should start feeling better, and what moving forward is supposed to look like.

This podcast is a permission slip for all of it.

Widow's Permission Slips is hosted by Gladys Jean Ullstam — certified life coach and Gary's widow. Gary died on the Saturday after Thanksgiving, November 27, 2021. He was 56 and a quarter years old. And Gladys is still, every single day, figuring out what it means to keep going without him.

Each episode, Gladys sits with you in one specific thing you might be experiencing — the exhaustion, the guilt, the identity questions, the impossible holidays, the moments nobody warned you about. Not to fix it. Not to rush you through it. Just to say: you are not alone in this, and you have permission.

Widow's Permission Slips is written primarily for widows — but if you have lost a spouse or partner and you are trying to find your footing, you belong here too.

New episodes drop regularly. Subscribe wherever you listen to podcasts.

Gladys Jean Ullstam is the founder of Ember & Bloom Coaching for Widows and a certified life coach. To book a free Holding the Ember discovery call, visit emberandbloomcoaching.com/appointments.

title: Heal Yeah!
author: mallorymarkowicz
description: Heal Yeah! Is the podcast where healing gets real, soul alignment gets easy, and your next level becomes reality. If you’re ready to leave old programming behind, and truly recognize and step into your own power- this is where you need to be. Take a breath, tune in, and say it with me… HEAL YEAH!

title: Mount Tamborine Christian Convention
author: Mt Tamborine Convention Centre
description: The Mount Tamborine Christian Convention is well known for trusted Bible Teaching.

title: What Comes Next
author: Level Access + Making Space + Advertising Week
description:
How do you win attention in 2026? The content breaking through isn't necessarily the most creative, it's the most personal. Yet up to 25% of your audience might not recognize themselves reflected in your campaigns. Many aren’t even able to access the products you’re promoting.

Despite representing a $675 billion consumer market segment, according to a 2026 Disability:IN study, Disabled consumers are often an afterthought. But they don’t have to be.

Welcome to What Comes Next, an Advertising Week limited series exploring how accessibility and authentic Disability representation are driving creativity and innovation. Hosted by Dana Randall, Executive Creative Director at Level Access, and Keely Cat-Wells, founder and CEO of Making Space, each episode will interview the marketers, advertisers, and designers behind accessible products and the campaigns that brought them to life.

New episodes every other Tuesday.

title: Hope & Daggers: A Daggerheart Adventure
author: Dagger Master Noah
description: An original Daggerheart adventure!

title: The Knighttime Cast
author: Knight
description: Enjoy a podcast full of stories, sports, and more. Episodes every week.

title: JXN's Table
author: Visit Jackson
description:
We gather for all sorts of reasons, not just to share a meal. We gather to create, connect, and celebrate. To dance, sing, shout, and let it all out. And sometimes, to sit back, relax, explore, and enjoy. We gather as a means of community. So, when we invite you to JXN’s Table, we're inviting you to experience something special, something only Jackson has: our people and our sense of place. 
Jackson’s culinary scene is shared across one table, and every restaurant here contributes to a larger, connected experience that defines the City With Soul.
So come, join us at the table, and stay awhile. Wherever you sit, you’re in for something good.

title: HawkEye 360 Salon Breakfast Podcast
author: Stacey Bruzzese
description: This podcast features discussion from industry experts in the space-enabled defense technology industry from The HawkEye 360 hosted breakfast events at Space Symposium.

title: Permanent Underpod
author: Jackson Cook
description: AI, stablecoins, Bitcoin, and the future of technology. Three tech veterans on a couch having the conversations we wish happened more often—deep dives, honest opinions, and plenty of debate.

title: Channel Bytes & Brews
author: FNG
description: The Podcast for IT Professionals, Channel Businesses and Technology Enthusiasts! Join Greg Upton, Rob White and Katy Upton from Fortitude Nicsa Global and join us on a journey where we will uncover emerging trends, business hints and tips and much more!

title: The Wonder Years Journey
author: Billy Smith
description: As a kid in the late ’90s, I discovered The Wonder Years on Nick at Nite and instantly fell in love with it. There was just one problem: I never got to see the whole series. Now, nearly thirty years later, I’m finally going back to watch every episode from beginning to end. Along the way, I’ll share memories, favorite moments, writing insights, and reflections on why I believe The Wonder Years is one of the greatest television shows ever made. Welcome to The Wonder Years Journey.

title: Nsight with Tommy Howard
author: The Norton Agency
description: Nsight with Tommy Howard brings you an inside look at the North Georgia real estate market with local insight, agent conversations, community trends, and real estate updates from the people living it every day. Powered by Norton.

title: The Real Reason You Feel Stuck in Life
author: Anubhav singh
description:
In this episode, we explore the real psychological reasons why people stay stuck in life, even when they want change.
This podcast will help you understand your habits, mindset, and hidden patterns that control your success. Listen till the end for the key insight that can change your thinking.

title: The Sales Garage
author: Luke Dodds
description: For woodworkers looking to scale their company profitably and sustainably, Luke Dodds covers how he scaled his company, The Timber Garage, as well as dozens of other businesses.

title: Ruthless Mothering
author: Yvette Curtis-Brown
description:
“So Monoah asked him, “…what kind of rules should govern the boys life and work?” (Judges 13:12) 



Welcome to Ruthless Mothering! 



Hi, I'm Yvette! I'm a homeschooling mom of three beautiful girls. Sometimes, I’ve felt like the epitome of an undeserving mom. Sometimes, I've messed up thoroughly, but I thank God for His help, teaching, discipline, and restoration and for choosing me to raise these God-loving girls!



Ruthless Mothering is about raising whole children, rather than piecing them out to the world. Moms, today, feel the need to be more vigilant and proactive than ever before. The Bible is our best Resource if we want them to grow up knowing how to live well in this world.
 


 I believe God understands the challenges moms face and wants to help us.
 
 

 As I was preparing Ruthless Mothering, a few questions that came to mind. 
 

 
 - Am I stewarding our family based on what God and His Word says or based on how my parents raised me, on trauma, or on what others are saying/doing? 
 
 

 - Who do I want my children see me pleasing more:  God or others? 
 

 
 - Do my children see me spending more time on my phone or with God and my family?
 
 

Ruthless Mothering (RM) is focused on being a mom God's way because His direction is the only one that counts. He created us, and He knows what He thinks about us. (Jeremiah 29:11)
 
 

 Thank you for your interest in Ruthless Mothering!
 
 

 Please remember to rate and review the Podcast! 
 

 
If you have show suggestions, comments, or questions, feel free to email me at ruthless@ruthlessmothering.com.

title: Outside Insight: Community-Funded Futures
author: Banach, Banach & Cassidy
description: Outside Insight: Community-Funded Futures is a podcast for public education and municipal leaders from Michigan-based Banach, Banach & Cassidy. BB&C is a trusted leader in strategic marketing, survey research, and finance campaign services. BB&C experts present solutions-based discussions with expert insight around marketing and public engagement strategies and strategic planning to help public institutions secure lasting support.

title: The Vertical Stride
author: Jack Wren
description:
Welcome to the Vertical Stride! The podcast where we break down best practices of the first-year runner all the way to the professional athlete.
Whether you've been in the sport for 30 years or just getting started, we have something for you!

title: Unhinged confessionals
author: Shana bell
description: You’ve entered Unhinged — the podcast that doesn’t play it safe. Hosted by Shana bell every episode pushes boundaries with bold opinions, wild stories, and zero apologies. If you’re tired of polished and predictable, it’s time to get a little Unhinged.

title: Unsolicited Dance Advice by Audrey De Bry, Smart Dancers Academy
author: Audrey De Bry
description:
The number one podcast for critical dancers who want answers.
I am Audrey De Bry, sports physiotherapist and former professional dancer. Every Wednesday I take a piece of dance advice everyone repeats and nobody questions, and I tell you why it is not helping you, and what actually would.
Expect strong opinions, real reasoning, and the occasional rant about a culture that trains dancers to ignore their bodies for fifteen years and then tells them to listen to them. Expect stories from close to ten years of working with professional dancers. Expect no injury-prevention lectures and no "just rest it." Mostly, expect to be treated like someone smart enough to handle the actual answer.
For dancers who are tired of nodding along. The science serves the art.

title: Minnesota Journal of International Law Presents
author: Minnesota Journal of International Law
description: The Official Podcast of the Minnesota Journal of International Law

title: Soul Unscripted
author: Rebecca Reinhart
description:
Soul Unscripted, with me, Rebecca Reinhart or Rebs The Wrecka  (healer, author, and guide).
This is the space where healing gets honest, spirituality gets grounded, it's always a little cheeky, and truth takes center stage.
Each episode dives into the raw, real, and often messy journey of becoming your truest self, probably with some additional sass. From emotional healing and intuitive development to conscious relationships and self-worth, this podcast is here to help you come home to your soul... unscripted, unfiltered, and fully alive.
Expect real talk, gentle truths, and spiritual insight that actually lands in your human experience. Some episodes are heart-to-heart reflections, while others open the mic to inspiring guests who bring fresh perspectives and depth.
Look out for special guests, including recurring “Naked Truths” episodes with Aubrey, where we get candid about healing, sensuality, embodiment, and the unspoken layers of growth we all move through.
Come as you are, leave more connected, and tune in for the kind of conversations that remind you: your soul already knows the way.

title: Benson Murder Case - A Philo Vance Story, The by S. S. Van Dine (1888 - 1939)
description:
The Benson Murder Case – A Philo Vance Story is the first of a series of twelve popular mysteries set in New York during the Jazz Age. S. S. Van Dine is the nom de plume of prominent art critic, and member of New York’s avant-garde, W. H. Wright. He rapidly became one of the country’s best-selling authors and the series remained immensely popular for decades, as Philo Vance was featured in dozens of movies, plays and radio shows.

Van Dine’s novels marked a sharp departure from earlier detective fiction. To begin with, the hero represents the antithesis of the familiar hard-boiled detective. He is an eccentric and volatile loner; a highly erudite aesthete; a debonair bon vivant; a fop. Indeed, Van Dine even flirts with his hero’s sexuality where, for instance, a friend tells Vance: “I trust you won’t wear your green carnation,” – then the symbol of homosexuality. Moreover, Philo Vance approaches crime from a totally new standpoint, more or less ignoring the sorts of evidence and inference generally used to solve mysteries. His perspective is primarily psychological. Thus, he tells his friend Van: “The truth can be learned only by an analysis of the psychological factors of a crime, and an application of them to the individual. The only real clues are psychological—not material.” (The author casts himself in the role of the narrator, “Van,” Vance’s old college friend, now his lawyer, advisor and general agent.)

Within minutes of viewing the scene of the crime, Vance throws out veiled hints and innuendos that he knows who murdered Alvin Benson. D. A. John Markham good-humoredly ignores these intimations and soon finds there is enough evidence to make an arrest, when Vance convinces him that his suspect could not possibly be guilty. After developing a strong evidential case against someone else, Vance proves that this second suspect, too, must be innocent. And so, it goes with several more suspects. In the end Vance identifies, and explains how his reasoning immediately pointed to, the actual murderer. (Summary by Kirsten Wever)

title: Space Moves You - Global
author: SMY
description: Space Moves You - Global brings you the most comprehensive updates from the final frontier. Utilizing a custom-built engine, we aggregate daily developments from over 15 world-class space and satellite news sources. These complex data points are then transformed into engaging, AI-driven conversations that explore not just the 'what,' but the 'why' behind space exploration. From orbital mechanics to the latest satellite launches, join our AI hosts as they decode the cosmos for the global space community. Stay informed, stay curious. Space moves us all.

title: Politics & Potables
author: Jon Buchwald
description: Politics and Potables is bringing civil political discourse back to where it started — face to face, over a drink, the way the Founders intended. Host Jon Buchwald, former congressional candidate and strategic advisor, invites guests to challenge him to a cocktail of their choosing, and then challenges them to an honest conversation about politics, policy, and what we actually have in common.

title: COME ALL YE CHILDREN
author: Christi Lannan
description: COME ALL YE CHILDREN will serve as devotionals for your little ones. Let's begin the journey of teaching our children to know and comprehend the Word of God.

title: Learn Science With Mujab
author: Learn Science With Mujab
description:
Have you ever stared at a massive skyscraper on a windy day and wondered why it doesn't just snap? Or looked up at the night sky and felt a sudden, freezing wave of absolute awe? The universe is full of hidden laws and silent mechanisms that rule our lives and honestly, they are way too fascinating to be left trapped in boring textbooks.

Welcome to Learn Science with Mujab, a podcast designed for the deeply curious mind. Consider this your late-night audio sanctuary where we break down complex concepts into completely grounded, cinematic stories. We don’t do hyperactive shouting, and we definitely don’t do dry, mind-numbing lectures. Instead, we dive into the deep mysteries of engineering triumphs, cosmic solitude, and the bizarre accidents of history that built our modern world.

 Think of it as the ultimate blend of psychological chills, atmospheric storytelling, and an effortless way to calm your mind after a long day. Every episode is a relaxed journey into something entirely new. Together, we will unpack the high-stakes, domino-effect engineering failures that forced human ingenuity to evolve under The Architecture of Error. We will journey into the mind-bending realities of planetary physics, the deep ocean abyss, and existential science with Deep Time & Cosmic Solitude. And we will uncover the eccentric geniuses and accidental discoveries behind the everyday tech we take for granted in Ghosts in the Machine.

Whether you're an engineering student needing a fresh perspective, a tech enthusiast, or just someone who loves putting on headphones to unwind and learn something mind-expanding before sleep,you’ve found your spot. Hit subscribe, get comfortable, and let’s learn something new together. Stay curious.

title: Stories To Be Told With Talitha
author: Talitha Vermeulen
description: Stories To Be Told With Talitha is a podcast where real people share the moments that shaped their lives. Hosted by 27-year-old mum Talitha, each episode explores powerful, honest conversations with guests from all walks of life — men and women with stories of joy, loss, resilience and growth. After learning her son Arlo was born blind, Talitha’s world changed, leading her to find her voice and create a space where no story feels too small or too heavy. This podcast is about connection, perspective and community — a place to feel seen, heard and a little less alone.

author: Altum Insight
description: Take a deep dive into qualitative research, AI, and the future of campaigns with Adrianne Marsh and Frank A. Spring, with producer Kate Monson. Altum Insight is a qualitative research firm that uses cognitive neuroscience and narrative science to understand voter behavior.

title: Sir Walter Scott (1771 - 1832) - The Black Dwarf
description: The Black Dwarf is the first of the “Tales of My Landlord” series, which also includes The Bride of Lammermoor and The Heart of Midlothian. The third preliminary chapter depicts two horsemen arriving at the Wallace Inn in Gandercleugh, where one of the horsemen relates the tale. The story proper is set on the Scottish borders during the reign of Queen Anne. The Jacobite movement is in its infancy, but the inhabitants of this part of the country have enough to quarrel about without politics inciting them to it. Several families of high and low birth bear no good intentions to each other, and a weird, brooding dwarf, who wishes ill on all of humanity, seems to direct and perpetuate the mayhem from his lonely cottage on Mucklestane Moor. (tbragg)

title: First Men in the Moon
description: Britain won the Moon Race! Decades before Neal Armstrong took his "giant leap for mankind" two intrepid adventurers from Lympne, England, journeyed there using not a rocket, but an antigravity coating. Mr. Bedford, who narrates the tale, tells of how he fell in with eccentric inventor Mr. Cavor, grew to believe in his researches, helped him build a sphere for traveling in space, and then partnered with him in an expedition to the Moon. What they found was fantastic! There was not only air and water, but the Moon was honeycombed with caverns and tunnels in which lived an advanced civilization of insect-like beings. While Bedford is frightened by them and bolts home, Cavor stays and is treated with great respect. So why didn't Armstrong and later astronauts find the evidence of all this? Well, according to broadcasts by Cavor over the newly-discovered radio technology, he told the Selenites too much about mankind, and apparently, they removed the welcome mat! (Summary by Mark)

title: Andrew Lang (1844 - 1912) and Sir Walter Scott (1771 - 1832) - The Antiquary
description: Illegitimacy, false identity, and bankruptcy are the major elements of Sir Walter Scott's 1816 novel, The Antiquary. Set in the period of the French Revolution, the novel's hero, Lovel, struggles to gain repute and the hand of his beloved despite his uncertain parentage. During these pursuits, he befriends the title's antiquary, Johnathan Oldbuck, who finds Lovel a captive audience to his scholarly studies and a tragic likeness to his own disappointments in love. Readers will discover whether Lovel's acts of bravery and courage ultimately earn him the birth and fortunes of a nobleman. (Summary by S. Kovalchik)

title: Blueprints of Progress: The Inventions That Built Our World
description: “Blueprints of Progress” is a captivating journey through the greatest inventions that shaped human civilization. From fire and steel to artificial intelligence and space travel, each episode reveals the fascinating stories, breakthroughs, and human struggles behind the tools that built our modern world. Dive deep into history, science, and innovation as we uncover how these creations redefined what it means to be human—and where they might take us next.

title: Sleeper Awakes
description: The Sleeper Awakes is a dystopian novel about a man who sleeps for two hundred years, waking up in a completely transformed London, where, because of compound interest, he has become the richest man in the world. He has been the famous Sleeper for centuries. A fanatic socialist, the main character awakes to see his nightmares realized, and the future revealed to him in all its horrors and malformities. The people adore him, and their masters - the supervisors of his legacy who rule in his name - do not want him breathing. (Summary by Stephan and Wikipedia)

title: War in the Air
description: War in the Air was written during a prolific time in H. G. Wells's writing career. Having withdrawn from British politics to spend more time on his own ideas, he published twelve books between 1901 and 1911, including this one. while many British citizens were surprised by the advent of World War I, Wells had already written prophetically about such a conflict. War in the Air predicted use of airplanes in modern war. (Summary by Bill Boerst)

title: In the Days of the Comet
description: William ("Willie") is a student living in the British town of Clayton. As a Socialist, he tries to move power from the upper class to the working class. Interestingly, in a fictitious confrontation Britain declares war on Germany. Willie falls in love with Nettie, but when she elopes with an upper-class man, Willie resolves to kill them both. Throughout the novel there is present in the sky a large comet which gives off a green glow. As Willie prepares to shoot the lovers, two battleships appear and begin shelling the coast, causing Willie to nearly lose his targets. As the comet enters the atmosphere, it gives off a green gas which envelopes everyone including Willie, who falls asleep. Willie wakes up a changed man. He is able to reason so clearly that he realizes the foolishness of his plan for revenge. Other people have changed too. Our hero marvels at how humankind has risen to new levels of vision and understanding. (Summary from Wikipedia)

author: Juliet

Juliet Diaries is your safe space for honest conversations about life, growth, relationships, faith, healing, and becoming the best version of yourself.

Hosted by Juliet, this podcast is like sitting down with a trusted friend who isn't afraid to talk about the real moments—the wins, the struggles, the lessons, and everything in between. Every episode is filled with heartfelt stories, practical advice, encouragement, and inspiration to help you navigate life's journey with confidence and purpose.

Whether you're chasing your dreams, overcoming challenges, building healthier relationships, or simply looking for motivation to keep going, Juliet Diaries is here to remind you that you're never alone.

Join me every week as we learn, grow, laugh, reflect, and write new chapters together—one diary entry at a time.

description: Neuroradiologist designing human durability through primal intelligence.

title: Tales of Space and Time
description: Five short Science Fiction stories. - Summary by Richard Kilmer

description: A collection of short science fiction stories, written by H.G. Wells. - Summary by Krista Zaleski

title: Coast to Coast Packers
author: Amar Desai
description: Talking all things Packers, NFL, and More

title: Door in the Wall, and Other Stories
description: A collection of short stories by H. G. Wells, author of "The Time Machine" and "War of the Worlds". The science fiction master ranges over a variety of topics, each original and unexpected. Included in this collection is "The Country of the Blind" where a man with sight hopes to make himself king. In other stories a stranger offers to sell diamonds on the street, a magic door appears requires a sacrifice to go through it, a demon machine tries to become a god, an engineer finds an engineering solution to a love triangle, and a man dreams or does he? - Summary by Beth Thomas

title: War of the Worlds (Version 3)
description: No one would have believed, in the last years of the nineteenth century, that the Earth was being scrutinised and studied from across the gulf of space. With infinite complacency, humanity went about its little affairs, serene in its assurance of its empire over matter. It is possible that the micro-organisms we watch under a microscope, do the same. Few people gave thought to the idea of life on other planets, and none imagined that it could be so vastly superior in intellect to ourselves. No one considered the possibility of extra-terrestrial danger. Yet the eyes that regarded our planet were envious and unsympathetic, and slowly and surely drew their plans against us. (Cori Samuel, adapted from Chapter One.)

title: Why Do Teeth Hurt Without a Cavity?
description: Welcome everyone to today's podcast. Have you ever felt tooth pain, rushed to the dentist, and then heard the surprising words, "You don't have a cavity"? It happens more often than people think. Today we're discussing why teeth can hurt even when there isn't any decay.

title: Highways in Hiding
description: Mechanical engineer Steve Cornell lives in a world where ESP and telepathy are normal, and a spreading plague called Mekstrom’s Disease turns its victims into supermen. In the search for his missing fiancée, Steve must discover who controls the disease and what they want with him. - Summary by Jessica Martin

title: The Architecture of the In Between
author: Michelle Jobes
description: A podcast for leaders navigating decisions where the path forward isn't clear yet. Host Michelle Jones shares The LENS Effect™ — a four-phase framework (Listen, Evaluate, Name, Select) built from 30+ years leading through high-stakes, high-consequence environments. Step into the in-between, and find your next aligned step.

title: Mummy and Miss Nitocris: A Phantasy of the Fourth Dimension
description: " Certain it should be that, beyond and about this World of Length, and Breadth, and Thickness, there is another World, or State of Existence, consisting of these and another dimension of which only those beings who are privileged to enter or dwell in it can have any conception. Now, if this postulate be granted, it follows that a dweller in this State would be freed from those conditions of Time and Space which bind those beings who are confined within the limits of Tri-Dimensional Space, or Existence. For example, he would be able to make himself visible or invisible to us at will by entering into or withdrawing himself from this State, and returning into that of Four Dimensions, whither our eyes could not follow him—even though he might be close to us in our sense of nearness. Moreover, he could be in two or more places at once, and cause two bodies to occupy the same space—which to us is inconceivable. Stranger still, he might be both alive and dead at the same time—since Past, Present, and Future would be all one to him; the world without beginning or end ..."—From the "Geometrical Possibilities," of Abd'el Kasir, of Cordoba, circa. 1050 A.D. ( from the Forward to The Mummy and Miss Nitocris)

title: Air Trust
description: A morphine-addicted billionaire, Isaac Flint, and his power-hungry partner, "Tiger" Waldron, are not sated with their vast wealth and plot world domination by controlling the world's oxygen supply through technological siphoning of the atmosphere. They'd sell the oxygen back, of course, for a premium, but make no mistake- they would cut off that oxygen for anyone who doesn't bow to them. In their way stands a socialist worker, Gabriel, who uncovers the plot and seeks to stop them. The billionaire's daughter, Catherine Flint, through a chance encounter with Gabriel, becomes entangled with the affair, too. Will plutocracy win out and enslave the human race, or will humanity throw off the shackles of those who would do so? (Summary by Louis Noyes)

title: Enormous Room
description: When an ethnically diverse group of people are suddenly transported from a rollercoaster in an amusement park to a stark and alien room peopled with giant ape-like creatures that want to jab at them with electric prods and use them for experiments, will they be able to band together to escape this nightmare situation? - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Story of the Days to Come
description: Set in London in the early 22nd century, this novella depicts the troubles of two lovers, Denton and Elizabeth. Elizabeth is a well-to-do woman with many connections, but Denton is only a middle-class worker. Elizabeth's father strongly disapproves of the match, and orders a hypnotist to hypnotize his daughter into thinking that she is in love with another. When the scheme is found out, Denton and Elizabeth elope, leading to a string of events that take them from the top of London society to horrifying depths of "underneath" London. (Summary by WoollyBee)

title: Benjamin Britten: Source Stories of Twelve Operas by Various
description: Britten's operas are firmly established in the international repertoire: according to Operabase, they are performed worldwide more than those of any other composer born in the 20th century, and only Puccini and Richard Strauss come ahead of him if the list is extended to all operas composed after 1900.  Britten went to various sources for his stories from the Bible to Japanese noh plays.  This is a collection of twelve of the source stories.  All but one are the original texts; the one exception is A Midsummer Night’s Dream which the reader took from Charles Lamb’s telling of the story.  Some operas are omitted because no public domain texts could be found for their sources. Summary by Wikipedia and David Wales

title: World Set Free
description: The World Set Free is a novel published in 1914 by H. G. Wells. The book is considered a prophetical novel foretelling the advent of nuclear weapons.A constant theme in Wells's work, such as his 1901 nonfiction book Anticipations, was the role of energy and technological advance as a determinant of human progress.The novel opens: "The history of mankind is the history of the attainment of external power. Man is the tool-using, fire-making animal." Scientists of the day were well aware that the slow natural radioactive decay of elements like radium continues for thousands of years, and that while the rate of energy release is negligible, the total amount released is huge. Wells used this as the basis for his story. (Summary on Wikipedia)

title: Invisible Man (Version 3)
description: A mysterious, bandaged stranger arrives in the small English village of Iping - and chaos follows close behind. H.G. Wells' classic story of obsession, murder, and science run amok. (Summary by Andrew Gaunce)

title: Benjamin Franklin by Robin McKown (1907 - 1975)
description: This biography of Franklin was written for young people, but can be enjoyed by anyone.  The author Robin McKown, is known for her young adult historical fiction and historical biographies. - Summary by Ciufi Galeazzi

title: Food of the Gods and How it Came to Earth
description: Two stuffy English scientists, always looking to further their scientific knowledge, create a substance called Herakleophorbia, which in its fourth incarnation – known as Herakleophorbia IV – has the special ability of making things increase greatly in size. As the scientists begin experimentation on some chicks, the substance is misused by some “country folk” who don’t take it seriously and soon Herakleophorbia IV is running rampant throughout England and then across the globe, creating giant plants and animals that wreak havoc on the land and then the people. Then the first giant babies are revealed and for the first time humanity has to contend with the existence of a new race of giant people. How humanity deals with this shocking new creation is revealed in The Food of the Gods and How it Came to Earth. (Summary by Alex C. Telander)

title: Time Machine (version 5)
description: Our dystopian future is revealed by H.G.Wells. In this famous story, an unnamed scientist of the 1800s invents a machine to travel through time. Going into the far future he discovers the human race has evolved into two distinct branches that are horrifyingly interdependent. Society, being divided into the upper and lower classes as it was at the time this story was written, has caused this horrifying thing to happen. - Summary by phil chenevert

title: Twelve Stories and a Dream
description: Thirteen short stories by HG Wells, the master of speculative fiction! Included in this collection is "Mr Skelmersdale in Fairyland" where a man finds his way into fairyland where a fairy queen tries to seduce him away from his human fiancée. In other stories a ghost gets stuck and can't get back to the "other side", a man decides to try being a god for a few months, a magic shop sells "the real thing", a scientist sells time in a bottle, a body is stolen (while its owner is still alive) and a man dreams or does he? (Summary by Beth Thomas)

title: Time Machine (Version 3)
description: H.G. Wells' classic science fiction-fantasy story, in which a scientist known only as “The Time Traveller” tells the tale of his journey to the year 802,701 A.D. and beyond, where he witnesses the end of human civilization as we know it, as well as the beginning of the end of the world. This original time-travel story has been copied many times, but never improved upon. (Summary by Mark Nelson)

title: Invisible Man
description: The Invisible Man (1897) is one of the most famous science fiction novels of all time. Written by H.G. Wells (1866-1946), it tells the story of a scientist who discovers the secret of invisibility and uses it on himself. The story begins as the Invisible Man, with a bandaged face and a heavy coat and gloves, takes a train to lodge in a country inn whilst he tries to discover the antidote and make himself visible again. The book inspired several films and is notable for its vivid descriptions of the invisible man--no mean feat, given that you can't see him!

title: Time Machine
description: The Time Machine is a novel by H. G. Wells, first published in 1895, later made into two films of the same title. This novel is generally credited with the popularization of the concept of time travel using a vehicle that allows an operator to travel purposefully and selectively. (Summary from wikipedia.org) Note: This audiobook contains readings of chapter 1 by two different readers. Variety is the spice of life.

title: Island of Doctor Moreau (Version 4)
description: The Island of Doctor Moreau by H. G. Wells follows shipwreck victim, Edward Prendick, as he explores a strange island his rescuer, Montgomery, has brought him to. The island’s host, Moreau, is a scientist whose life purpose is to push the limits of science, creating strange humanoid animals. Prendick wrestles with morality as his hosts continue their strange experimentations. (Summary by Veronica Mead)

title: Stolen Bacillus and other stories
description: A collection of 15 humorous short stories by the original master of speculative fiction: H. G. Wells. This was the first collection of short stories published by the author, and contains a mixture of fantasy, science-fiction and humour! - Summary by Beth Thomas

title: Benito Cereno by Herman Melville (1819 - 1891)
description: On an island off the coast of Chile, Captain Amaso Delano, sailing an American sealer, sees the San Dominick, a Spanish slave ship, in obvious distress. Capt. Delano boards the San Dominick, providing needed supplies, and tries to learn from her aloof and disturbed captain, Benito Cereno, the story of how this ship came to be where she is. Dealing with racism, the slave trade, madness, the tension between representation and reality, and featuring at least one unreliable narrator, Melville's novella has both captivated and frustrated critics for decades. (Summary by Nullifidian)

title: Men Like Gods
description: In the summer of 1921, a disenchanted journalist escapes the rat race for a drive in the country. But Mr. Barnstaple's trip exceeds his expectations when he and other motorists are swept 3,000 years into the future. The inadvertent time travelers arrive in a world that corresponds exactly to Barnstaple's ideals: a utopian state, free of crime, poverty, war, disease, and bigotry. Unfettered by the constraints of government and organized religion, the citizens lead rich, meaningful lives, passed in pursuit of their creative fancies. Barnstaple's traveling companions, however, quickly contrive a scheme to remake the utopia in the image of their twentieth-century world. - Summary that was published with the book originally

title: D-99
description: Five citizens of Terra were being held on other worlds -- and the ultra-secret Department 99 existed only to set them, and others like them, free. (Summary from text)

title: Benigna Machiavelli by Charlotte Perkins Gilman (1860 - 1935)
description:
In between "The Yellow Wallpaper" (1892) and Herland (1915), feminist Charlotte Perkins Gilman (1860-1935) wrote and published this delightful fictional autobiography, Benigna Machiavelli (1914), in her monthly magazine, The Forerunner.

The narrator, young Benigna MacAvelly, decides as a child that she intends to emulate her ancestor Niccolò Machiavelli but dedicate her machinations to doing good rather than evil. She starts her ingenious plotting very early in life (for example, as an 11-year-old, she enlists her classmates in an elaborate money-raising scheme to buy a new watch for an impoverished teacher), and moves on to larger goals as she gets older. Her most significant challenge is her domineering father. Determined to liberate her downtrodden mother from his verbal abuse, Benigna concocts an elaborate plan to deal with him and restore her mother's self-confidence. (Summary by Winnifred Assmann)

title: Blue Star
description: The novel is set in a parallel world in which the existence of psychic powers has permitted the development of witchcraft into a science; in contrast, the physical sciences have languished, resulting in a modern culture reminiscent of our eighteenth century. The protagonists are Lalette Asterhax, a hereditary witch, and Rodvard Bergelin, an ordinary government clerk who has been recruited into the radical conspiracy of the Sons of the New Day. Rodvard, though attracted to the daughter of a baron, is commanded by his superiors to seduce Lalette instead to gain the use of her blue star in the furtherance of their revolutionary aims. The witch is no more truly enamored of him than he is of her, but both fall in with the scheme for their own reasons, unaware of how much they are simply pawns in the larger scheme of things. (Summary adapted from Wikipedia)

title: Dream: a novel
description: The story is set in a Utopian future. On an excursion, Sarnac, relates his vivid dream to his friends and recounts how he lived the entire life of one Harry Mortimer Smith, an Englishman from times past, in his dream. - Summary by Sonia

title: Oomphel in the Sky
description: Natives of the distant planet of Kwannon believe that their world is about to end, and in preparing for the apocalypse, may be unnecessarily bringing about their own demise. The planetary government can’t overcome its own bureaucracy to help them, and the military is overwhelmed. Can a single newsman change the course of a whole people, and save their world? (by Mark Nelson)

title: Benighted by John Boynton Priestley (1894 - 1984)
description:
Phillip and Margaret Waverton, along with a cheerful young acquaintance Roger Penderel, finds themselves driving through a terrible storm with no solace to be found. Fortunately they espy a craggy, old house on a hill right as the road they are on is washed away, and they stop to take shelter for the night. Inhabiting this house are the peculiar Femms, a wiry, creaking old man and his blustery, deaf sister, attended to by a grotesque, hulking brute of a mute butler named Morgan. They are told the master of the house, Sir Roderick, is confined upstairs in bed. What bizarre dark secrets will be uncovered on this haunting and mysterious night?
Thrill to Priestley's classic of gothic literature which was adapted into the influential film The Old Dark House in 1932 and again in 1963. - Summary by Ben Tucker

title: Omnilingual
description: An expedition to Mars discovers the remains of an advanced civilization, which died out many thousands of years ago. They recovered books and documents left behind, and are puzzled by their contents. Would the team find their “Rosetta Stone” that would allow them to unlock the Martian language, and learn the secrets of this long-dead race? (Summary by Mark Nelson)

title: Olga Romanoff
description: Olga Romanoff (1894) is a science fiction novel by the English writer George Griffith, first published as The Syren of the Skies in Pearson's Weekly. The novel continues (from The Angel of the Revolution) the tale of a worldwide brotherhood of anarchists fighting the world armed with fantastical airships, ending on an apocalyptic note as a comet smashes into the earth. - Summary by Wikipedia

title: Chessmen of Mars
description: The Chessmen of Mars is a science fiction novel by the mental sword and physical pen wielding author Edgar Rice Burroughs. It the fifth installment of his Barsoom series. The Chessmen of Mars is a tale of wonder and excellence that follows John Carter and Dejah Thoris, along with their daughter Tara, princess of Helium, as she is sought after by Gahan, Jed of Gathol. Adventure and perile ensue as the story unfolds. (Summary by tubeyes) Note: The Prelude and Chapter 1 are read by two different readers. Variety is the spice of life.

title: Put The Bucket In The Bucket
author: Gareth and Noel
description:
Your life works. So why doesn't it feel like it?
Most people spend their lives chasing happiness, success, love, healing, purpose, or enlightenment.
Stop chasing. Start listening.
PTBITB is a conversation between two brothers exploring emotional survival, loneliness, identity, fear, awakening, and the stories we mistake for ourselves.
No gurus. No self-help hacks. Just the conversation you have with yourself at 3am—out loud.
If you've built a good life and still feel something is missing, welcome.

title: Preferred Risk
description: The Company insures you against everything. Everything except war, that is. But they've put an end to wars (or so they claim). The Company also controls everything. Including all the sources of weapons. The Company is dedicated to the happiness of mankind (or so they claim). Medical Treatment and Law Enforcement are just a few of the other services they provide to the entire world. Claims Adjuster Wills was a happy Company employee until his path crossed those of a man with no legs and a mysterious woman. All of a sudden, his world was turned upside down, and his decisions could determine the future of the planet. This collaborative work was originally published under the pseudonym Edson McCann. It was the winner of the 1955 Galaxy-Simon & Schuster novel contest. - Summary by Nick Bulka

title: Wonderful Visit
description: An other-worldly creature visits a small English village, and H. G. Wells uses humour and satire to convey some of the imperfections of Victorian society, as ‘angel’ and humans view each other with equal incomprehension.( summary by Mary Bard)

title: Omnilingual (version 2)
description: This short story was first written and published in the year 1957, as part of a collection of short science fiction stories in the collection Astounding Science Fiction. Piper's story is unusual in focusing on the problem of archaeology on an alien culture. How is is possible to decipher writings of an alien race that died out 50,000 years ago? What could we possibly have in common with them? There can be no 'Rosetta Stone' with a shared language so is it impossible? That is the struggle of the protagonist and against all odds she does indeed find a way for our two cultures to meet. An excellent story well written. - Summary by Phil chenevert

title: Naudsonce
description: Naudsonce? What does THAT mean? Well, to find out you will need to listen to this story where Piper's unique mind explores what we mean by 'communication' and how it happens. The joint Space Navy-Colonial Office expedition was looking for new planets suitable for colonization; they had been out, now, for four years, which was close to maximum for an exploring expedition. They had entered eleven systems, and made landings on eight planets. Three had been reasonably close to Terra-type but were all disqualified by terrible animals or warlike inhabitants. Now, finally here was an ideal world; their last chance before returning in disgrace. Now the only thing was to get an agreement from the local king or whatever to the colonization. Easy, right? Well first, you've got to talk to them ...... and there the trouble starts. Listen to this captivating story by H. Beam Piper published in Analog Science Fact—Science Fiction, January 1962.

title: Omnilingual (Version 3)
description: This short story published in 1957 is unusual for its time in that its protagonist is a female scientist, and there is no romantic subplot. Helped by some of her teammates on the Mars exploration crew, and hindered by others, she struggles to decipher the meanings of the artifacts left by an extinct alien species. What possible frame of reference can humans and the long-gone Martians have in common? The answer is very satisfying in this well told tale. - Summary by Trish E. Matson

title: Ministry of Disturbance
description: Science Fiction at it humorous best written in the golden age of all the greats and published in Astounding Science Fiction December 1958. Piper can't be matched for sardonic comment on human nature. The Empire is stagnant; thousands of planets spread around Earth but nothing new is allowed to happen and disturb the static situation. Our emperor Paul knows this but is powerless to make a dent in the centuries old hide bound bureaucracy of intergalactic politics. But is there a plot to overthrow him? It certainly looks like it. Follow this story closely as it builds to an excellent and exciting climax. Piper's talent and creativity shine through in this story.

title: Last Enemy
description: An undercover Paratimer has disappeared on assignment while in an alternate time line, and it’s up to Verkan Vall of the Paratime Police to save her. To do so, he must infiltrate a universe in which assassination is an honorable profession, and reincarnation a scientific fact. Will Verkan Vall survive in a world of killers and the undead? (Summary by Mark Nelson)

title: Behind the Drop
author: Cash Drop Media
description: Behind the Drop is a behind-the-scenes look at building a local media brand from the ground up. Join us as we share the wins, mistakes, ideas, and strategies behind growing 423 Cash Drop, expanding into new cities, partnering with local businesses, and creating experiences that bring communities together. If you’re interested in entrepreneurship, social media growth, local marketing, or simply following the journey, this is where you’ll hear it all as it happens.

title: Edge of the Knife
description: The Terro-Human Future History is Piper's detailed account of the next 6000 years of human history. 1942, the year the first fission reactor was constructed, is defined as the year 1 A.E. (Atomic Era). In 1973, a nuclear war devastates the planet, eventually laying the groundwork for the emergence of a Terran Federation, once humanity goes into space and develops antigravity technology. The story "The Edge of the Knife" (collected in Empire) occurs slightly before the war, and involves a man who sees flashes of the future. It links many key elements of Piper's series. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Temple Trouble
description: Did you know that our little earth is not limited to the single time line on which we happen to live? There are actually thousands, no millions of parallel times, each existing alongside all the rest. Some extremely primitive, some very advanced, bot all unaware of the others. Of course this does not affect us at all, unless ... unless one of these time lines discovers the existence of the others and then a way to move easily back and forth from one to the other. Then a few problems might happen. Well, it has happened and the ParaPolice are kept busy sorting out things like what happened here ...

title: Five Sci-Fi Short Stories by H. Beam Piper
description: Five short stories by classic science fiction writer H. Beam Piper.

title: Police Operation
description: H. Beam Piper (1904–1964) was an American science fiction author. He wrote many short stories and several novels. He is best known for his extensive Terro-Human Future History series of stories and a shorter series of "Paratime" alternate history tales. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Tethered to Life
author: gusdaguy00
description: Jacob Guy invites normal, every day people to share their struggles with suicidal thoughts or their experience with losing someone to suicide.

title: Slave Is a Slave
description: The Galactic Empire is slowly 'welcoming' into the family of civilized worlds those systems so far off in the backwater of the galaxy that they have been overlooked and ignored for the past 500 years or so. This is purely routine work because every planet offered the chance has eagerly accepted the invitation. Mainly because the enlightened Empire lets the planetary government continue to rule and do whatever it wants...with a few minor restrictions of course; and because the they are shown what happens to planets who decide not to accept the invitation.Aditya is the system in question here. Forgotten for almost a millennium but surviving, thank you very much, with an economy based entirely on slavery. Everyone is a slave except for the few thousand Lords Master. What happens when the this type of society meets the Constitution of the Galactic Empire which forbids any type of slavery? What happens when the poor, oppressed slaves are given their freedom? Funny you should ask ....

title: Heads of Cerberus
description: A pioneering work in the alternate worlds genre, The Heads of Cerberus was serialized in The Thrill Book in 1919 and published as a novel in 1952. A vial of grey dust transports three unwitting time travelers to a totalitarian version of Philadelphia in the year 2118. While certain landmarks stand unchanged, the societal structure is unrecognizable. Superlative adjectives and numbered buttons have replaced names and an oligarchy wield godlike power over the masses. To return home, the trio must win over the Loveliest, outsmart the Cleverest, and survive a deadly competition. - Summary by Christina Fu

title: Cosmic Computer
description: Conn Maxwell returns from Terra to his poverty-stricken home planet of Poictesme, "The Junkyard Planet", with news of the possible location of Merlin, a military super-computer rumored to have been abandoned there after the last war. The inhabitants hope to find Merlin, which they think will be their ticket to wealth and prosperity. But is Merlin real, or just an old rumor? And if they find it will it save them, or tear them apart? (by Mark Nelson)

title: Looking Backward: 2000-1887
description: Looking Backward: 2000-1887 is a utopian novel by Edward Bellamy, first published in 1888. It was the third largest bestseller of its time, after Uncle Tom's Cabin and Ben-Hur: A Tale of the Christ.The book tells the story of Julian West, a young American who, towards the end of the 19th century, falls into a deep, hypnosis-induced sleep and wakes up more than a century later. He finds himself in the same location (Boston, Massachusetts) but in a totally changed world: It is the year 2000 and, while he was sleeping, the U.S.A. has been transformed into a socialist utopia. This book outlines Bellamy's complex thoughts about improving the future, and is an indictment of industrial capitalism. (Summary adapted from Wikipedia)

title: Time Crime
description: The Paratime Police had a real headache this time! Tracing one man in a population of millions is easy--compared to finding one gang hiding out on one of billions of probability lines! (Summary by Gutenberg text)

title: Uller Uprising
description: Uller Uprising is the story of a confrontation between a human overlord and alien servants, with an ironic twist at the end. Like most of Piper's best work, Uller Uprising is modeled after an actual event in human history; in this case the Sepoy Mutiny (a Bengal uprising in British-held India brought about when rumors were spread to native soldiers that cartridges being issued by the British were coated with animal fat. The rebellion quickly spread throughout India and led to the massacre of the British Colony at Cawnpore.). Piper's novel is not a mere retelling of the Indian Mutiny, but rather an analysis of an historical event applied to a similar situation in the far future. (Summary from John F. Carr's introduction)

title: Little Fuzzy
description: Jack Holloway, a prospector on the planet Zarathustra discovers small furry creatures. These creatures are obviously intelligent, but are they animals or are they sapient? If they are sapient the planet will be declared a protected zone and the company that is developing the planet commercially will lose their exclusive rights to the resources... (Summary by tabithat)

title: Four-Day Planet
description: Fenris isn't a hell planet, but it's nobody's bargain. With 2,000-hour days and an 8,000-hour year, it alternates blazing heat with killing cold. A planet like that tends to breed a special kind of person: tough enough to stay alive and smart enough to make the best of it. When that kind of person discovers he's being cheated of wealth he's risked his life for, that kind of planet is ripe for revolution. (Introduction from the Gutenberg text)

title: Anglo-American Alliance: A Serio-Comic Romance and Forecast of the Future
description: Described by io9 as “the first lesbian science fiction novel,” An Anglo-American Alliance is a quasi-farcical tale of love, transformation, and geopolitics set in the far-flung futuristic year of 1960. In it, the titular Anglo-American Alliance has established itself as the world government, ushering in new age of technological and social revolution. However, even in this halcyon period, the “love that dare not speak its name” remains an anathema. The novel’s central narrative follows the long-burgeoning but secret romance between two women at a ladies’ seminary school in Cornwall: Margaret MacDonald and Aurora Cunningham. Throughout this tale, we follow their trials and tribulations as they grapple with secret longings and learn how to harness the powers of science and technology to make their dreams come true.
